## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Next: spec-restore-cult-verb-procedural-specs (₢BBAAZ) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Restorative spec authoring. BBAAQ Surface 2 inlined six cult-verb operations
  into RBS0 (lines 1045-1119) as 5-7 line abstract behavioral contracts and
  left the original procedural sub-spec files (RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL)
  un-included on disk. The orphans hold detail the new RBS0 inline content
  does not: API endpoint URLs, HTTP response code expectations,
  `axhopa_arg_parameter` / `axhos_step` / `axhoot_typed_output` /
  `axhoc_completion` blocks, liturgical links to Google REST docs.
  
  This pace re-authors the procedural specifications so the new cult-verb
  operations sit on the same depth tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM / RBSDV /
  RBSDY (full procedural sub-specs included by RBS0), AND preserves git
  ancestry of the orphans by using `git mv` rather than create-then-delete.
  The orphans are still on disk un-deleted, so the rename path is open;
  preserving blame/log on the procedural detail is a load-bearing recovery
  of project history.
  
  Pure .adoc — no code, no narrative tracks — but with judgment work on
  each behavioral-contract adaptation.
  
  Runs after BBAAY (regime-spec restructure) so the depot-identity rewrite
  landing pattern is settled when the new procedural specs reference RBRR
  fields.
  
  ## Source orphans → target operations (with ancestry strategy)
  
  The mapping is asymmetric: knight specs map 1:1 to invest specs (clean
  ancestry preservation), but sa_forfeit and sa_list each split per-role
  (ancestry preserved on one side of each split, second side authored
  fresh).
  
  | Source orphan | Target file (via `git mv`) | Ancestry-preserved? | Fresh-authored sibling |
  |---------------|----------------------------|---------------------|------------------------|
  | RBSDK-director_knight.adoc | RBSDK-director_invest.adoc | yes | — |
  | RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc | RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc | yes | — |
  | RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc | RBSDD-director_divest.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc (fresh) |
  | RBSSL-sa_list.adoc | RBSDR-director_roster.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc (fresh) |
  
  Acronym suggestions are subject to executor verification against terminal
  exclusivity (CLAUDE.md prefix discipline) — RBSDD / RBSRD / RBSDR / RBSRL
  are candidate mints; if any collide, executor picks alternates and
  documents the choice.
  
  Director-side claims the rename for splits because:
  - D < R alphabetically gives a stable convention
  - Equivalent choice; only constraint is consistency across the two splits
  
  ## Two-stage commit strategy (git-mv ancestry)
  
  Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
  robust against wholesale content replacement:
  
  **Stage 1 — pure rename, content unchanged:**
  ```
  git mv RBSDK-director_knight.adoc RBSDK-director_invest.adoc
  git mv RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc
  git mv RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc RBSDD-director_divest.adoc
  git mv RBSSL-sa_list.adoc RBSDR-director_roster.adoc
  ```
  Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace
  each new path back through the rename to the orphan's full history.
  
  **Stage 2 — content rewrite per-file:**
  Each renamed file gets its content replaced to match the new
  behavioral contract (see "Behavioral-contract deltas" below). Per-file
  commits keep diffs reviewable. Content replacement is wholesale — old
  knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics fully gone — but the file path
  through which history is traced remains intact.
  
  **Stage 3 — author fresh siblings:**
  RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc are written
  new. Content shape mirrors the director-side sibling (which got the
  ancestry), with role-specific adjustments. These two have no git
  ancestry by necessity — one source can't ancestry-bear two targets.
  
  ## Behavioral-contract deltas (from RBS0:1045-1119)
  
  Each new spec must reflect these BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts, which differ
  from the orphans' originals:
  
  **invest** (was knight/charter):
  - Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator
  - SA name derived from role + depot moniker (regime-kindled), not operator-supplied
  - Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively;
    the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires
  - Fatal if SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key
  
  **divest** (was sa_forfeit, now per-role):
  - Zero-arg by-role lookup: reads role's installed RBRA, extracts SA email,
    issues REST DELETE
  - After successful cloud DELETE, removes role's production RBRA file to
    mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse
  
  **roster** (was sa_list, now per-role):
  - Read-only observation
  - Emits one fact file per discovered SA via BUS0 multi-fact convention
    (`buf_write_fact_multi` against a registered extension constant); fact
    content is empty, presence-as-fact
  - Stdout-substring assertions retire from consumer-facing surface
  
  ## Procedural detail to preserve (from orphans)
  
  Each new spec carries forward the orphans' shape:
  
  - `//axhob_operation` opener with operation quoin
  - NOTE block describing the operation's role and scope
  - `//axhopa_arg_parameter` / `//axhopt_typed_parameter` blocks (or omit
    parameters where the new contract is zero-arg)
  - `//axhos_step` blocks with `{rbbc_*}` action verbs (`{rbbc_call}`,
    `{rbbc_require}`, `{rbbc_store}`, `{rbbc_fatal}`, `{rbbc_show}`)
  - API endpoint URLs (link form: `link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/...[...]`)
  - HTTP response code expectations (200 / 404 handling, etc.)
  - `//axhoot_typed_output` block declaring output type
  - `//axhoc_completion` closing prose
  
  Match the depth and shape of RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc as the gold standard
  for a procedural sub-spec.
  
  ## RBS0 restructure
  
  Once the six new sub-spec files exist:
  
  - Anchors `[[rbtgo_director_invest]]` etc. move from RBS0 inline content
    (currently lines 1045-1119) into the new sub-spec files (each file's
    `axhob_operation` opener carries the anchor).
  - RBS0 lines 1045-1119 are replaced by `include::RBSDK-director_invest.adoc[]`
    / etc. directives, plus any cross-cutting prose that was inline (the
    preamble at 1039-1043 about cult-verb triad and colophon shape may stay
    inline as cross-cutting framing).
  - Mapping section unchanged (the six attribute references already exist
    at RBS0:117-132).
  
  ## Orphan disposal
  
  Replaced by ancestry-preserving rename strategy above. No `git rm`
  required — the orphans transform into the new files via `git mv` (four
  of the six) plus two fresh siblings. CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings
  section updates to reflect:
  - RBSDK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
  - RBSRK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
  - RBSSF and RBSSL entries removed (renamed away)
  - New entries added for RBSDD, RBSRD, RBSDR, RBSRL (or alternates if
    executor mints differently)
  
  ## Companion sweep: RBSRR axho_* prose discipline
  
  RBSRR's three operations paragraphs (`axhro_kindle` / `axhro_validate` /
  `axhro_render`) currently leak BUK helper names (`buv_*`, `ZRBRR_*`)
  into prose — surfaced during BBAAY notch `fc815a27` review (the followup
  landed on top of the original docket-scoped `07730eba` and over-corrected
  stale field references into bash-routine references). Sweep with the same
  concept-language + linked-term discipline being applied to the new
  procedural specs above. Field references via linked terms
  (`{rbrr_vessel_dir}` etc.), not bare uppercase. The 30-char GCP project
  ID joint-length cap and the operator-visible section-name list
  enumerated by `axhro_render` (Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local
  Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration,
  Secrets Directory, Public Docs) stay — they describe operator-visible
  output, not implementation.
  
  ## Verification
  
  - Six new sub-spec files exist with full procedural detail (axho* blocks,
    API endpoints, HTTP codes).
  - `git log --follow` on each of the four ancestry-preserved files shows
    history reaching back through the rename to the orphan's full log.
  - RBS0 includes all six via `include::` directives.
  - AsciiDoc rendering of RBS0 shows the new operations at the same depth
    tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM.
  - `grep -n 'rbtgo_director_invest\|rbtgo_director_divest\|rbtgo_director_roster\|rbtgo_retriever_invest\|rbtgo_retriever_divest\|rbtgo_retriever_roster'`
    shows anchors live in sub-spec files; mapping references in RBS0; xref
    consumers (RBSGS post-BBAAV) resolve correctly.
  - `grep -E 'buv_|ZRBRR_' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc`
    returns zero hits (companion-sweep success).
  - CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings section updated.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Code changes to rbgg_Governor.sh, rbgi_IAM.sh, or any bash module
  - Behavioral changes to operations beyond the BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts
  - Changes to other operations' specs (RBSDE, RBSDU, RBSGM, etc.)
  - Tabtarget files
  - Test fixtures
  
  ## References
  
  - BBAAQ commit b73ae194 (wrap) — Surface 2 commit 202c7726 inlined the
    abstracts; orphan disposal explicitly framed as deferred to BBAAN.
  - BBAAV commit 021aff0f — companion mechanical xref sweep; surfaced the
    RBS0:1045-1108 inline content for operator review.
  - BBAAY notch fc815a27 — RBSRR axho_* paragraphs landed bash-routine
    leakage that the companion sweep above repairs.
  - RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc — gold-standard procedural sub-spec shape.
  - Orphan files RBSDK / RBSRK / RBSSF / RBSSL — still on disk un-deleted;
    the inputs to the rename strategy.

RBCC sources before RBGD in every CLI that consumes RBGD, so source-time availability is preserved. (2) Add cult-verb tinder triad RBCC_verb_invest/RBCC_verb_divest/RBCC_verb_roster, establishing the verb-name centralization that the upcoming SA-management surface (rbgg invest/divest/roster functions, six new colophons) will reference uniformly. Tinder-on-tinder expansion is now permitted — a tinder value may interpolate ${«PREFIX»_lower_name} for any tinder constant defined earlier in the same module's tinder block, since that expansion resolves at source time and preserves source-time availability. The original rule conflated the desired property (no runtime coupling) with a stricter mechanism (no ${} characters anywhere), forcing literal duplication of common substrings (role names, verb names) across tinder values that had no semantic justification. f3cd71c4  ₢BBAAM    Cycle 4 of BBAAM verification: complete the AUAAr base64 standardization that left decode sites broken on all platforms. Pristine-lifecycle case 3 (rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle) advanced past the BBAAS over-gate (Cycle 2) and the missing buf_fact source (Cycle 3), through 11 of 12 mantle steps successfully (depot validation, SA list, governor SA creation, IAM owner grant, keys.create with 200), then died at 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format' with 'Empty client_email in key JSON' — because the decoded key file rbgp_governor_key.json was 0 bytes despite a 3192-byte privateKeyData arriving from Google's keys.create endpoint. Root cause: openssl enc -base64 -d silently produces empty output (exit 0) when input is single-line base64 without PEM-style ~76-char line breaks. Documented OpenSSL behavior across all variants tested (https://wiki.openssl.org/index.php/Base64): 'If there is no newline on decode, it silently returns no data, which can be confusing; ... on commandline enc specify -A'. Empirically verified across three platforms / three OpenSSL variants: macOS LibreSSL 3.3.6 (system /usr/bin/openssl), macOS OpenSSL 3.5.2 (Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/openssl), Linux Ubuntu OpenSSL 3.0.13 (cerebro /usr/bin/openssl) — all three produce 0 bytes WITHOUT -A and full correct output WITH -A for single-line input. -A flag is documented in OpenSSL master docs, 1.1.1 docs, AND OpenBSD man pages (LibreSSL); cross-implementation stable since at least OpenSSL 1.0.0c. Why the bug went latent until now: pre-AUAAr (commit 385589f4, April 1, 2026), the code used 'base64 -d' (GNU) with 'base64 -D' (BSD) fallback — both GNU and BSD base64 silently tolerate long single-line input, so mantle worked everywhere. AUAAr's 'Standardize base64 encoding/decoding to openssl enc -base64 across 6 files' commit message captures the standardization intent but missed adding -A to decode sites. Encode sites already use -A correctly with documenting comment ('Base64url encode: -A suppresses line wrapping' at rbgo_OAuth.sh:67) — the project pattern was understood for encode, decode sites just got missed in AUAAr. Bug only triggers on actual SA key-decode against live Google API responses (single-line base64); operators rebuild SAs rarely, so dormant April 1 → today; pristine-lifecycle case 3 is the first systematic exercise of the path. User confirmed 'maybe I haven't rebuilt SAs in a while' — dormancy fits the timeline exactly. Repair (Maximal scope per operator nod): added -A to all four 'openssl enc -base64 -d' decode sites — (1) rbgp_Payor.sh:1355 (governor mantle key decode, case-3 blocking); (2) rbgg_Governor.sh:242 (sibling SA invest decode, would have blocked next cycle on invest paths); (3-4) rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:959,1187 (build provenance decodes, latent until conjure/bind/graft exercised — same root fault). Single-flag addition per site; matches project's own encode-side pattern; cross-binary verified. The fault was universal not platform-specific; all four sites were latent on every platform; Maximal-scope repair eliminates the entire bug class in one notch rather than letting other paths re-surface it. Five sites total in Tools/rbk/ have 'openssl enc -base64 -d' — the fifth (vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh:228) is in FUTURE/, not active code, deliberately not touched. Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Restorative spec authoring. BBAAQ Surface 2 inlined six cult-verb operations
  into RBS0 (lines 1045-1119) as 5-7 line abstract behavioral contracts and
  left the original procedural sub-spec files (RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL)

  does not: API endpoint URLs, HTTP response code expectations,
  `axhopa_arg_parameter` / `axhos_step` / `axhoot_typed_output` /
  `axhoc_completion` blocks, liturgical links to Google REST docs.
  
  This pace re-authors the procedural specifications so the new cult-verb
  operations sit on the same depth tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM / RBSDV /
  RBSDY (full procedural sub-specs included by RBS0), AND preserves git
  ancestry of the orphans by using `git mv` rather than create-then-delete.
  The orphans are still on disk un-deleted, so the rename path is open;
  preserving blame/log on the procedural detail is a load-bearing recovery
  of project history.
  
  Pure .adoc — no code, no narrative tracks — but with judgment work on
  each behavioral-contract adaptation.
  
  Runs after BBAAY (regime-spec restructure) so the depot-identity rewrite
  landing pattern is settled when the new procedural specs reference RBRR
  fields.
  
  ## Source orphans → target operations (with ancestry strategy)
  
  The mapping is asymmetric: knight specs map 1:1 to invest specs (clean
  ancestry preservation), but sa_forfeit and sa_list each split per-role
  (ancestry preserved on one side of each split, second side authored
  fresh).
  
  | Source orphan | Target file (via `git mv`) | Ancestry-preserved? | Fresh-authored sibling |
  |---------------|----------------------------|---------------------|------------------------|
  | RBSDK-director_knight.adoc | RBSDK-director_invest.adoc | yes | — |
  | RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc | RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc | yes | — |
  | RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc | RBSDD-director_divest.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc (fresh) |
  | RBSSL-sa_list.adoc | RBSDR-director_roster.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc (fresh) |
  
  Acronym suggestions are subject to executor verification against terminal
  exclusivity (CLAUDE.md prefix discipline) — RBSDD / RBSRD / RBSDR / RBSRL
  are candidate mints; if any collide, executor picks alternates and
  documents the choice.
  
  Director-side claims the rename for splits because:
  - D < R alphabetically gives a stable convention
  - Equivalent choice; only constraint is consistency across the two splits
  
  ## Two-stage commit strategy (git-mv ancestry)
  
  Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
  robust against wholesale content replacement:
  
  **Stage 1 — pure rename, content unchanged:**
  ```
  git mv RBSDK-director_knight.adoc RBSDK-director_invest.adoc
  git mv RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc
  git mv RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc RBSDD-director_divest.adoc
  git mv RBSSL-sa_list.adoc RBSDR-director_roster.adoc
  ```
  Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace
  each new path back through the rename to the orphan's full history.
  
  **Stage 2 — content rewrite per-file:**
  Each renamed file gets its content replaced to match the new
  behavioral contract (see "Behavioral-contract deltas" below). Per-file
  commits keep diffs reviewable. Content replacement is wholesale — old
  knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics fully gone — but the file path
  through which history is traced remains intact.
  
  **Stage 3 — author fresh siblings:**
  RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc are written
  new. Content shape mirrors the director-side sibling (which got the
  ancestry), with role-specific adjustments. These two have no git
  ancestry by necessity — one source can't ancestry-bear two targets.
  
  ## Behavioral-contract deltas (from RBS0:1045-1119)
  
  Each new spec must reflect these BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts, which differ
  from the orphans' originals:
  
  **invest** (was knight/charter):
  - Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator
  - SA name derived from role + depot moniker (regime-kindled), not operator-supplied
  - Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively;
    the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires
  - Fatal if SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key
  
  **divest** (was sa_forfeit, now per-role):
  - Zero-arg by-role lookup: reads role's installed RBRA, extracts SA email,
    issues REST DELETE
  - After successful cloud DELETE, removes role's production RBRA file to
    mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse
  
  **roster** (was sa_list, now per-role):
  - Read-only observation
  - Emits one fact file per discovered SA via BUS0 multi-fact convention
    (`buf_write_fact_multi` against a registered extension constant); fact
    content is empty, presence-as-fact
  - Stdout-substring assertions retire from consumer-facing surface
  
  ## Procedural detail to preserve (from orphans)
  
  Each new spec carries forward the orphans' shape:
  
  - `//axhob_operation` opener with operation quoin
  - NOTE block describing the operation's role and scope
  - `//axhopa_arg_parameter` / `//axhopt_typed_parameter` blocks (or omit
    parameters where the new contract is zero-arg)
  - `//axhos_step` blocks with `{rbbc_*}` action verbs (`{rbbc_call}`,
    `{rbbc_require}`, `{rbbc_store}`, `{rbbc_fatal}`, `{rbbc_show}`)
  - API endpoint URLs (link form: `link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/...[...]`)
  - HTTP response code expectations (200 / 404 handling, etc.)
  - `//axhoot_typed_output` block declaring output type
  - `//axhoc_completion` closing prose
  
  Match the depth and shape of RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc as the gold standard
  for a procedural sub-spec.
  
  ## RBS0 restructure
  
  Once the six new sub-spec files exist:
  
  - Anchors `[[rbtgo_director_invest]]` etc. move from RBS0 inline content
    (currently lines 1045-1119) into the new sub-spec files (each file's
    `axhob_operation` opener carries the anchor).
  - RBS0 lines 1045-1119 are replaced by `include::RBSDK-director_invest.adoc[]`
    / etc. directives, plus any cross-cutting prose that was inline (the
    preamble at 1039-1043 about cult-verb triad and colophon shape may stay
    inline as cross-cutting framing).
  - Mapping section unchanged (the six attribute references already exist
    at RBS0:117-132).
  
  ## Orphan disposal
  
  Replaced by ancestry-preserving rename strategy above. No `git rm`
  required — the orphans transform into the new files via `git mv` (four
  of the six) plus two fresh siblings. CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings
  section updates to reflect:
  - RBSDK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
  - RBSRK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
  - RBSSF and RBSSL entries removed (renamed away)
  - New entries added for RBSDD, RBSRD, RBSDR, RBSRL (or alternates if
    executor mints differently)
  
  ## Companion sweep: RBSRR axho_* prose discipline
  
  RBSRR's three operations paragraphs (`axhro_kindle` / `axhro_validate` /
  `axhro_render`) currently leak BUK helper names (`buv_*`, `ZRBRR_*`)
  into prose — surfaced during BBAAY notch `fc815a27` review (the followup
  landed on top of the original docket-scoped `07730eba` and over-corrected
  stale field references into bash-routine references). Sweep with the same
  concept-language + linked-term discipline being applied to the new
  procedural specs above. Field references via linked terms
  (`{rbrr_vessel_dir}` etc.), not bare uppercase. The 30-char GCP project
  ID joint-length cap and the operator-visible section-name list
  enumerated by `axhro_render` (Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local
  Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration,
  Secrets Directory, Public Docs) stay — they describe operator-visible
  output, not implementation.
  
  ## Verification
  
  - Six new sub-spec files exist with full procedural detail (axho* blocks,
    API endpoints, HTTP codes).
  - `git log --follow` on each of the four ancestry-preserved files shows
    history reaching back through the rename to the orphan's full log.
  - RBS0 includes all six via `include::` directives.
  - AsciiDoc rendering of RBS0 shows the new operations at the same depth
    tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM.
  - `grep -n 'rbtgo_director_invest\|rbtgo_director_divest\|rbtgo_director_roster\|rbtgo_retriever_invest\|rbtgo_retriever_divest\|rbtgo_retriever_roster'`
    shows anchors live in sub-spec files; mapping references in RBS0; xref
    consumers (RBSGS post-BBAAV) resolve correctly.
  - `grep -E 'buv_|ZRBRR_' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc`
    returns zero hits (companion-sweep success).
  - CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings section updated.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Code changes to rbgg_Governor.sh, rbgi_IAM.sh, or any bash module
  - Behavioral changes to operations beyond the BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts
  - Changes to other operations' specs (RBSDE, RBSDU, RBSGM, etc.)
  - Tabtarget files
  - Test fixtures
  
  ## References

    the inputs to the rename strategy.

f3cd71c4  ₢BBAAM    Cycle 4 of BBAAM verification: complete the AUAAr base64 standardization that left decode sites broken on all platforms. Pristine-lifecycle case 3 (rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle) advanced past the BBAAS over-gate (Cycle 2) and the missing buf_fact source (Cycle 3), through 11 of 12 mantle steps successfully (depot validation, SA list, governor SA creation, IAM owner grant, keys.create with 200), then died at 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format' with 'Empty client_email in key JSON' — because the decoded key file rbgp_governor_key.json was 0 bytes despite a 3192-byte privateKeyData arriving from Google's keys.create endpoint. Root cause: openssl enc -base64 -d silently produces empty output (exit 0) when input is single-line base64 without PEM-style ~76-char line breaks. Documented OpenSSL behavior across all variants tested (https://wiki.openssl.org/index.php/Base64): 'If there is no newline on decode, it silently returns no data, which can be confusing; ... on commandline enc specify -A'. Empirically verified across three platforms / three OpenSSL variants: macOS LibreSSL 3.3.6 (system /usr/bin/openssl), macOS OpenSSL 3.5.2 (Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/openssl), Linux Ubuntu OpenSSL 3.0.13 (cerebro /usr/bin/openssl) — all three produce 0 bytes WITHOUT -A and full correct output WITH -A for single-line input. -A flag is documented in OpenSSL master docs, 1.1.1 docs, AND OpenBSD man pages (LibreSSL); cross-implementation stable since at least OpenSSL 1.0.0c. Why the bug went latent until now: pre-AUAAr (commit 385589f4, April 1, 2026), the code used 'base64 -d' (GNU) with 'base64 -D' (BSD) fallback — both GNU and BSD base64 silently tolerate long single-line input, so mantle worked everywhere. AUAAr's 'Standardize base64 encoding/decoding to openssl enc -base64 across 6 files' commit message captures the standardization intent but missed adding -A to decode sites. Encode sites already use -A correctly with documenting comment ('Base64url encode: -A suppresses line wrapping' at rbgo_OAuth.sh:67) — the project pattern was understood for encode, decode sites just got missed in AUAAr. Bug only triggers on actual SA key-decode against live Google API responses (single-line base64); operators rebuild SAs rarely, so dormant April 1 → today; pristine-lifecycle case 3 is the first systematic exercise of the path. User confirmed 'maybe I haven't rebuilt SAs in a while' — dormancy fits the timeline exactly. Repair (Maximal scope per operator nod): added -A to all four 'openssl enc -base64 -d' decode sites — (1) rbgp_Payor.sh:1355 (governor mantle key decode, case-3 blocking); (2) rbgg_Governor.sh:242 (sibling SA invest decode, would have blocked next cycle on invest paths); (3-4) rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:959,1187 (build provenance decodes, latent until conjure/bind/graft exercised — same root fault). Single-flag addition per site; matches project's own encode-side pattern; cross-binary verified. The fault was universal not platform-specific; all four sites were latent on every platform; Maximal-scope repair eliminates the entire bug class in one notch rather than letting other paths re-surface it. Five sites total in Tools/rbk/ have 'openssl enc -base64 -d' — the fifth (vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh:228) is in FUTURE/, not active code, deliberately not touched. Verification deferred to retry-after-notch in a fresh chat (operator request: prompt for clean-chat resumption to be issued).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
f6f12d5f  ₢BBAAM    Cycle 3 of BBAAM verification: rbgp_cli.sh missing buf_fact.sh source. Pristine-lifecycle case 2 (rbtdrp_depot_lifecycle), now reaching past the BBAAS over-gate fixed in Cycle 2, advanced through pre-levy depot_list and successful levy of depot prlc-d-pristq100000 in GCP, then died at the post-levy depot_list re-probe with 'Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh: line 311: buf_write_fact_multi: command not found' (exit 127). Diagnosis: buz_exec_lookup (Tools/buk/buz_zipper.sh:275) does 'BUZ_FOLIO=... exec ...' to launch the target CLI, replacing the bash process. Functions sourced earlier in bud_dispatch.sh:301 ('source buf_fact.sh') do NOT survive that exec boundary. Each CLI that calls buf_write_fact_multi or buf_write_fact_single must source buf_fact.sh in its own furnish chain. Sibling CLIs that already do: rbob_cli.sh:127, rbfd_cli.sh:33, rbfl_cli.sh:33 — those CLIs were updated when BBAAO landed the new BUF function. BBAAS reshaped rbgp_Payor.sh to call buf_write_fact_multi (lines 311-312, depot_list state emit) and buf_write_fact_single (line 1303, governor mantle SA email fact) but missed adding the source line to rbgp_cli.sh. Same furnish-gap pattern as Cycles 1 and 2: BBAAS landed the consumer-side change, didn't propagate the dependency-source change. GCP state at notch: depot prlc-d-pristq100000 ACTIVE with mason-pristq100000 SA present (gcloud-verified) — levy succeeded, only the post-levy re-probe failed. Fixture tolerates this by design: rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker per BBAAS scans depot_list output and allocates next free six-digit (pristq100001 next). No cleanup needed. Repair: insert 'source ${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh' into zrbgp_furnish (rbgp_cli.sh:37, after buh_handbook.sh, before rbgc_Constants.sh) — mirrors sibling-CLI source ordering. One-line addition. Next: spec-restore-cult-verb-procedural-specs (₢BBAAZ) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Restorative spec authoring. BBAAQ Surface 2 inlined six cult-verb operations
  into RBS0 (lines 1045-1119) as 5-7 line abstract behavioral contracts and
  left the original procedural sub-spec files (RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL)
  un-included on disk. The orphans hold detail the new RBS0 inline content
  does not: API endpoint URLs, HTTP response code expectations,
  `axhopa_arg_parameter` / `axhos_step` / `axhoot_typed_output` /
  `axhoc_completion` blocks, liturgical links to Google REST docs.
  
  This pace re-authors the procedural specifications so the new cult-verb
  operations sit on the same depth tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM / RBSDV /
  RBSDY (full procedural sub-specs included by RBS0), AND preserves git
  ancestry of the orphans by using `git mv` rather than create-then-delete.
  The orphans are still on disk un-deleted, so the rename path is open;
  preserving blame/log on the procedural detail is a load-bearing recovery
  of project history.
  
  Pure .adoc — no code, no narrative tracks — but with judgment work on
  each behavioral-contract adaptation.
  
  Runs after BBAAY (regime-spec restructure) so the depot-identity rewrite
  landing pattern is settled when the new procedural specs reference RBRR
  fields.
  
  ## Source orphans → target operations (with ancestry strategy)
  
  The mapping is asymmetric: knight specs map 1:1 to invest specs (clean
  ancestry preservation), but sa_forfeit and sa_list each split per-role
  (ancestry preserved on one side of each split, second side authored
  fresh).
  
  | Source orphan | Target file (via `git mv`) | Ancestry-preserved? | Fresh-authored sibling |
  |---------------|----------------------------|---------------------|------------------------|
  | RBSDK-director_knight.adoc | RBSDK-director_invest.adoc | yes | — |
  | RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc | RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc | yes | — |
  | RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc | RBSDD-director_divest.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc (fresh) |
  | RBSSL-sa_list.adoc | RBSDR-director_roster.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc (fresh) |
  
  Acronym suggestions are subject to executor verification against terminal
  exclusivity (CLAUDE.md prefix discipline) — RBSDD / RBSRD / RBSDR / RBSRL
  are candidate mints; if any collide, executor picks alternates and
  documents the choice.
  
  Director-side claims the rename for splits because:
  - D < R alphabetically gives a stable convention
  - Equivalent choice; only constraint is consistency across the two splits
  
  ## Two-stage commit strategy (git-mv ancestry)
  
  Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
  robust against wholesale content replacement:
  
  **Stage 1 — pure rename, content unchanged:**
  ```
  git mv RBSDK-director_knight.adoc RBSDK-director_invest.adoc
  git mv RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc
  git mv RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc RBSDD-director_divest.adoc
  git mv RBSSL-sa_list.adoc RBSDR-director_roster.adoc
  ```
  Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace
  each new path back through the rename to the orphan's full history.
  
  **Stage 2 — content rewrite per-file:**
  Each renamed file gets its content replaced to match the new
  behavioral contract (see "Behavioral-contract deltas" below). Per-file
  commits keep diffs reviewable. Content replacement is wholesale — old
  knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics fully gone — but the file path
  through which history is traced remains intact.
  
  **Stage 3 — author fresh siblings:**
  RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc are written
  new. Content shape mirrors the director-side sibling (which got the
  ancestry), with role-specific adjustments. These two have no git
  ancestry by necessity — one source can't ancestry-bear two targets.
  
  ## Behavioral-contract deltas (from RBS0:1045-1119)
  
  Each new spec must reflect these BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts, which differ
  from the orphans' originals:
  
  **invest** (was knight/charter):
  - Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator
  - SA name derived from role + depot moniker (regime-kindled), not operator-supplied
  - Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively;
    the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires
  - Fatal if SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key
  
  **divest** (was sa_forfeit, now per-role):
  - Zero-arg by-role lookup: reads role's installed RBRA, extracts SA email,
    issues REST DELETE
  - After successful cloud DELETE, removes role's production RBRA file to
    mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse
  
  **roster** (was sa_list, now per-role):
  - Read-only observation
  - Emits one fact file per discovered SA via BUS0 multi-fact convention
    (`buf_write_fact_multi` against a registered extension constant); fact
    content is empty, presence-as-fact
  - Stdout-substring assertions retire from consumer-facing surface
  
  ## Procedural detail to preserve (from orphans)
  
  Each new spec carries forward the orphans' shape:
  
  - `//axhob_operation` opener with operation quoin
  - NOTE block describing the operation's role and scope
  - `//axhopa_arg_parameter` / `//axhopt_typed_parameter` blocks (or omit
    parameters where the new contract is zero-arg)
  - `//axhos_step` blocks with `{rbbc_*}` action verbs (`{rbbc_call}`,
    `{rbbc_require}`, `{rbbc_store}`, `{rbbc_fatal}`, `{rbbc_show}`)
  - API endpoint URLs (link form: `link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/...[...]`)
  - HTTP response code expectations (200 / 404 handling, etc.)
  - `//axhoot_typed_output` block declaring output type
  - `//axhoc_completion` closing prose
  
  Match the depth and shape of RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc as the gold standard
  for a procedural sub-spec.
  
  ## RBS0 restructure
  
  Once the six new sub-spec files exist:
  
  - Anchors `[[rbtgo_director_invest]]` etc. move from RBS0 inline content
    (currently lines 1045-1119) into the new sub-spec files (each file's
    `axhob_operation` opener carries the anchor).
  - RBS0 lines 1045-1119 are replaced by `include::RBSDK-director_invest.adoc[]`
    / etc. directives, plus any cross-cutting prose that was inline (the
    preamble at 1039-1043 about cult-verb triad and colophon shape may stay
    inline as cross-cutting framing).
  - Mapping section unchanged (the six attribute references already exist
    at RBS0:117-132).
  
  ## Orphan disposal
  
  Replaced by ancestry-preserving rename strategy above. No `git rm`
  required — the orphans transform into the new files via `git mv` (four
  of the six) plus two fresh siblings. CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings
  section updates to reflect:
  - RBSDK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
  - RBSRK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
  - RBSSF and RBSSL entries removed (renamed away)
  - New entries added for RBSDD, RBSRD, RBSDR, RBSRL (or alternates if
    executor mints differently)
  
  ## Companion sweep: RBSRR axho_* prose discipline
  
  RBSRR's three operations paragraphs (`axhro_kindle` / `axhro_validate` /
  `axhro_render`) currently leak BUK helper names (`buv_*`, `ZRBRR_*`)
  into prose — surfaced during BBAAY notch `fc815a27` review (the followup
  landed on top of the original docket-scoped `07730eba` and over-corrected
  stale field references into bash-routine references). Sweep with the same
  concept-language + linked-term discipline being applied to the new
  procedural specs above. Field references via linked terms
  (`{rbrr_vessel_dir}` etc.), not bare uppercase. The 30-char GCP project
  ID joint-length cap and the operator-visible section-name list
  enumerated by `axhro_render` (Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local
  Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration,
  Secrets Directory, Public Docs) stay — they describe operator-visible
  output, not implementation.
  
  ## Verification
  
  - Six new sub-spec files exist with full procedural detail (axho* blocks,
    API endpoints, HTTP codes).
  - `git log --follow` on each of the four ancestry-preserved files shows
    history reaching back through the rename to the orphan's full log.
  - RBS0 includes all six via `include::` directives.
  - AsciiDoc rendering of RBS0 shows the new operations at the same depth
    tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM.
  - `grep -n 'rbtgo_director_invest\|rbtgo_director_divest\|rbtgo_director_roster\|rbtgo_retriever_invest\|rbtgo_retriever_divest\|rbtgo_retriever_roster'`
    shows anchors live in sub-spec files; mapping references in RBS0; xref
    consumers (RBSGS post-BBAAV) resolve correctly.
  - `grep -E 'buv_|ZRBRR_' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc`
    returns zero hits (companion-sweep success).
  - CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings section updated.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Code changes to rbgg_Governor.sh, rbgi_IAM.sh, or any bash module
  - Behavioral changes to operations beyond the BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts
  - Changes to other operations' specs (RBSDE, RBSDU, RBSGM, etc.)
  - Tabtarget files
  - Test fixtures
  
  ## References
  
  - BBAAQ commit b73ae194 (wrap) — Surface 2 commit 202c7726 inlined the
    abstracts; orphan disposal explicitly framed as deferred to BBAAN.
  - BBAAV commit 021aff0f — companion mechanical xref sweep; surfaced the
    RBS0:1045-1108 inline content for operator review.
  - BBAAY notch fc815a27 — RBSRR axho_* paragraphs landed bash-routine
    leakage that the companion sweep above repairs.
  - RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc — gold-standard procedural sub-spec shape.
  - Orphan files RBSDK / RBSRK / RBSSF / RBSSL — still on disk un-deleted;
    the inputs to the rename strategy.

f3cd71c4  ₢BBAAM    Cycle 4 of BBAAM verification: complete the AUAAr base64 standardization that left decode sites broken on all platforms. Pristine-lifecycle case 3 (rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle) advanced past the BBAAS over-gate (Cycle 2) and the missing buf_fact source (Cycle 3), through 11 of 12 mantle steps successfully (depot validation, SA list, governor SA creation, IAM owner grant, keys.create with 200), then died at 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format' with 'Empty client_email in key JSON' — because the decoded key file rbgp_governor_key.json was 0 bytes despite a 3192-byte privateKeyData arriving from Google's keys.create endpoint. Root cause: openssl enc -base64 -d silently produces empty output (exit 0) when input is single-line base64 without PEM-style ~76-char line breaks. Documented OpenSSL behavior across all variants tested (https://wiki.openssl.org/index.php/Base64): 'If there is no newline on decode, it silently returns no data, which can be confusing; ... on commandline enc specify -A'. Empirically verified across three platforms / three OpenSSL variants: macOS LibreSSL 3.3.6 (system /usr/bin/openssl), macOS OpenSSL 3.5.2 (Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/openssl), Linux Ubuntu OpenSSL 3.0.13 (cerebro /usr/bin/openssl) — all three produce 0 bytes WITHOUT -A and full correct output WITH -A for single-line input. -A flag is documented in OpenSSL master docs, 1.1.1 docs, AND OpenBSD man pages (LibreSSL); cross-implementation stable since at least OpenSSL 1.0.0c. Why the bug went latent until now: pre-AUAAr (commit 385589f4, April 1, 2026), the code used 'base64 -d' (GNU) with 'base64 -D' (BSD) fallback — both GNU and BSD base64 silently tolerate long single-line input, so mantle worked everywhere. AUAAr's 'Standardize base64 encoding/decoding to openssl enc -base64 across 6 files' commit message captures the standardization intent but missed adding -A to decode sites. Encode sites already use -A correctly with documenting comment ('Base64url encode: -A suppresses line wrapping' at rbgo_OAuth.sh:67) — the project pattern was understood for encode, decode sites just got missed in AUAAr. Bug only triggers on actual SA key-decode against live Google API responses (single-line base64); operators rebuild SAs rarely, so dormant April 1 → today; pristine-lifecycle case 3 is the first systematic exercise of the path. User confirmed 'maybe I haven't rebuilt SAs in a while' — dormancy fits the timeline exactly. Repair (Maximal scope per operator nod): added -A to all four 'openssl enc -base64 -d' decode sites — (1) rbgp_Payor.sh:1355 (governor mantle key decode, case-3 blocking); (2) rbgg_Governor.sh:242 (sibling SA invest decode, would have blocked next cycle on invest paths); (3-4) rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:959,1187 (build provenance decodes, latent until conjure/bind/graft exercised — same root fault). Single-flag addition per site; matches project's own encode-side pattern; cross-binary verified. The fault was universal not platform-specific; all four sites were latent on every platform; Maximal-scope repair eliminates the entire bug class in one notch rather than letting other paths re-surface it. Five sites total in Tools/rbk/ have 'openssl enc -base64 -d' — the fifth (vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh:228) is in FUTURE/, not active code, deliberately not touched. 5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Operator scope
55	
56	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
57	
58	## Coupling to ₣A_
59	
60	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
61	
62	## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)
63	
64	These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
65	
66	**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.
67	
68	**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.
69	
70	**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:
71	
72	- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
73	- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
74	- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
75	- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`
76	
77	**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.
78	
79	**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.
80	
81	**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.
82	
83	**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).
84	
85	## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split
86	
87	JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.
88	
89	**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.
90	
91	**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).
92	
93	**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.
94	
95	**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.
96	
97	## References
98	
99	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
100	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
101	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
102	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
103	
104	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAZ
105	
106	## Character
107	
108	Restorative spec authoring. BBAAQ Surface 2 inlined six cult-verb operations
109	into RBS0 (lines 1045-1119) as 5-7 line abstract behavioral contracts and
110	left the original procedural sub-spec files (RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL)
111	un-included on disk. The orphans hold detail the new RBS0 inline content
112	does not: API endpoint URLs, HTTP response code expectations,
113	`axhopa_arg_parameter` / `axhos_step` / `axhoot_typed_output` /
114	`axhoc_completion` blocks, liturgical links to Google REST docs.
115	
116	This pace re-authors the procedural specifications so the new cult-verb
117	operations sit on the same depth tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM / RBSDV /
118	RBSDY (full procedural sub-specs included by RBS0), AND preserves git
119	ancestry of the orphans by using `git mv` rather than create-then-delete.
120	The orphans are still on disk un-deleted, so the rename path is open;
121	preserving blame/log on the procedural detail is a load-bearing recovery
122	of project history.
123	
124	Pure .adoc — no code, no narrative tracks — but with judgment work on
125	each behavioral-contract adaptation.
126	
127	Runs after BBAAY (regime-spec restructure) so the depot-identity rewrite
128	landing pattern is settled when the new procedural specs reference RBRR
129	fields.
130	
131	## Source orphans → target operations (with ancestry strategy)
132	
133	The mapping is asymmetric: knight specs map 1:1 to invest specs (clean
134	ancestry preservation), but sa_forfeit and sa_list each split per-role
135	(ancestry preserved on one side of each split, second side authored
136	fresh).
137	
138	| Source orphan | Target file (via `git mv`) | Ancestry-preserved? | Fresh-authored sibling |
139	|---------------|----------------------------|---------------------|------------------------|
140	| RBSDK-director_knight.adoc | RBSDK-director_invest.adoc | yes | — |
141	| RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc | RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc | yes | — |
142	| RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc | RBSDD-director_divest.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc (fresh) |
143	| RBSSL-sa_list.adoc | RBSDR-director_roster.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc (fresh) |
144	
145	Acronym suggestions are subject to executor verification against terminal
146	exclusivity (CLAUDE.md prefix discipline) — RBSDD / RBSRD / RBSDR / RBSRL
147	are candidate mints; if any collide, executor picks alternates and
148	documents the choice.
149	
150	Director-side claims the rename for splits because:
151	- D < R alphabetically gives a stable convention
152	- Equivalent choice; only constraint is consistency across the two splits
153	
154	## Two-stage commit strategy (git-mv ancestry)
155	
156	Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
157	robust against wholesale content replacement:
158	
159	**Stage 1 — pure rename, content unchanged:**
160	```
161	git mv RBSDK-director_knight.adoc RBSDK-director_invest.adoc
162	git mv RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc
163	git mv RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc RBSDD-director_divest.adoc
164	git mv RBSSL-sa_list.adoc RBSDR-director_roster.adoc
165	```
166	Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace
167	each new path back through the rename to the orphan's full history.
168	
169	**Stage 2 — content rewrite per-file:**
170	Each renamed file gets its content replaced to match the new
171	behavioral contract (see "Behavioral-contract deltas" below). Per-file
172	commits keep diffs reviewable. Content replacement is wholesale — old
173	knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics fully gone — but the file path
174	through which history is traced remains intact.
175	
176	**Stage 3 — author fresh siblings:**
177	RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc are written
178	new. Content shape mirrors the director-side sibling (which got the
179	ancestry), with role-specific adjustments. These two have no git
180	ancestry by necessity — one source can't ancestry-bear two targets.
181	
182	## Behavioral-contract deltas (from RBS0:1045-1119)
183	
184	Each new spec must reflect these BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts, which differ
185	from the orphans' originals:
186	
187	**invest** (was knight/charter):
188	- Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator
189	- SA name derived from role + depot moniker (regime-kindled), not operator-supplied
190	- Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively;
191	  the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires
192	- Fatal if SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key
193	
194	**divest** (was sa_forfeit, now per-role):
195	- Zero-arg by-role lookup: reads role's installed RBRA, extracts SA email,
196	  issues REST DELETE
197	- After successful cloud DELETE, removes role's production RBRA file to
198	  mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse
199	
200	**roster** (was sa_list, now per-role):
201	- Read-only observation
202	- Emits one fact file per discovered SA via BUS0 multi-fact convention
203	  (`buf_write_fact_multi` against a registered extension constant); fact
204	  content is empty, presence-as-fact
205	- Stdout-substring assertions retire from consumer-facing surface
206	
207	## Procedural detail to preserve (from orphans)
208	
209	Each new spec carries forward the orphans' shape:
210	
211	- `//axhob_operation` opener with operation quoin
212	- NOTE block describing the operation's role and scope
213	- `//axhopa_arg_parameter` / `//axhopt_typed_parameter` blocks (or omit
214	  parameters where the new contract is zero-arg)
215	- `//axhos_step` blocks with `{rbbc_*}` action verbs (`{rbbc_call}`,
216	  `{rbbc_require}`, `{rbbc_store}`, `{rbbc_fatal}`, `{rbbc_show}`)
217	- API endpoint URLs (link form: `link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/...[...]`)
218	- HTTP response code expectations (200 / 404 handling, etc.)
219	- `//axhoot_typed_output` block declaring output type
220	- `//axhoc_completion` closing prose
221	
222	Match the depth and shape of RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc as the gold standard
223	for a procedural sub-spec.
224	
225	## RBS0 restructure
226	
227	Once the six new sub-spec files exist:
228	
229	- Anchors `[[rbtgo_director_invest]]` etc. move from RBS0 inline content
230	  (currently lines 1045-1119) into the new sub-spec files (each file's
231	  `axhob_operation` opener carries the anchor).
232	- RBS0 lines 1045-1119 are replaced by `include::RBSDK-director_invest.adoc[]`
233	  / etc. directives, plus any cross-cutting prose that was inline (the
234	  preamble at 1039-1043 about cult-verb triad and colophon shape may stay
235	  inline as cross-cutting framing).
236	- Mapping section unchanged (the six attribute references already exist
237	  at RBS0:117-132).
238	
239	## Orphan disposal
240	
241	Replaced by ancestry-preserving rename strategy above. No `git rm`
242	required — the orphans transform into the new files via `git mv` (four
243	of the six) plus two fresh siblings. CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings
244	section updates to reflect:
245	- RBSDK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
246	- RBSRK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
247	- RBSSF and RBSSL entries removed (renamed away)
248	- New entries added for RBSDD, RBSRD, RBSDR, RBSRL (or alternates if
249	  executor mints differently)
250	
251	## Companion sweep: RBSRR axho_* prose discipline
252	
253	RBSRR's three operations paragraphs (`axhro_kindle` / `axhro_validate` /
254	`axhro_render`) currently leak BUK helper names (`buv_*`, `ZRBRR_*`)
255	into prose — surfaced during BBAAY notch `fc815a27` review (the followup
256	landed on top of the original docket-scoped `07730eba` and over-corrected
257	stale field references into bash-routine references). Sweep with the same
258	concept-language + linked-term discipline being applied to the new
259	procedural specs above. Field references via linked terms
260	(`{rbrr_vessel_dir}` etc.), not bare uppercase. The 30-char GCP project
261	ID joint-length cap and the operator-visible section-name list
262	enumerated by `axhro_render` (Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local
263	Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration,
264	Secrets Directory, Public Docs) stay — they describe operator-visible
265	output, not implementation.
266	
267	## Verification
268	
269	- Six new sub-spec files exist with full procedural detail (axho* blocks,
270	  API endpoints, HTTP codes).
271	- `git log --follow` on each of the four ancestry-preserved files shows
272	  history reaching back through the rename to the orphan's full log.
273	- RBS0 includes all six via `include::` directives.
274	- AsciiDoc rendering of RBS0 shows the new operations at the same depth
275	  tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM.
276	- `grep -n 'rbtgo_director_invest\|rbtgo_director_divest\|rbtgo_director_roster\|rbtgo_retriever_invest\|rbtgo_retriever_divest\|rbtgo_retriever_roster'`
277	  shows anchors live in sub-spec files; mapping references in RBS0; xref
278	  consumers (RBSGS post-BBAAV) resolve correctly.
279	- `grep -E 'buv_|ZRBRR_' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc`
280	  returns zero hits (companion-sweep success).
281	- CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings section updated.
282	
283	## Out of scope
284	
285	- Code changes to rbgg_Governor.sh, rbgi_IAM.sh, or any bash module
286	- Behavioral changes to operations beyond the BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts
287	- Changes to other operations' specs (RBSDE, RBSDU, RBSGM, etc.)
288	- Tabtarget files
289	- Test fixtures
290	
291	## References
292	
293	- BBAAQ commit b73ae194 (wrap) — Surface 2 commit 202c7726 inlined the
294	  abstracts; orphan disposal explicitly framed as deferred to BBAAN.
295	- BBAAV commit 021aff0f — companion mechanical xref sweep; surfaced the
296	  RBS0:1045-1108 inline content for operator review.
297	- BBAAY notch fc815a27 — RBSRR axho_* paragraphs landed bash-routine
298	  leakage that the companion sweep above repairs.
299	- RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc — gold-standard procedural sub-spec shape.
300	- Orphan files RBSDK / RBSRK / RBSSF / RBSSL — still on disk un-deleted;
301	  the inputs to the rename strategy.
302
