# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split

JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.

**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.

**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).

**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.

**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAZ

## Character

Restorative spec authoring. BBAAQ Surface 2 inlined six cult-verb operations
into RBS0 (lines 1045-1119) as 5-7 line abstract behavioral contracts and
left the original procedural sub-spec files (RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL)
un-included on disk. The orphans hold detail the new RBS0 inline content
does not: API endpoint URLs, HTTP response code expectations,
`axhopa_arg_parameter` / `axhos_step` / `axhoot_typed_output` /
`axhoc_completion` blocks, liturgical links to Google REST docs.

This pace re-authors the procedural specifications so the new cult-verb
operations sit on the same depth tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM / RBSDV /
RBSDY (full procedural sub-specs included by RBS0), AND preserves git
ancestry of the orphans by using `git mv` rather than create-then-delete.
The orphans are still on disk un-deleted, so the rename path is open;
preserving blame/log on the procedural detail is a load-bearing recovery
of project history.

Pure .adoc — no code, no narrative tracks — but with judgment work on
each behavioral-contract adaptation.

Runs after BBAAY (regime-spec restructure) so the depot-identity rewrite
landing pattern is settled when the new procedural specs reference RBRR
fields.

## Source orphans → target operations (with ancestry strategy)

The mapping is asymmetric: knight specs map 1:1 to invest specs (clean
ancestry preservation), but sa_forfeit and sa_list each split per-role
(ancestry preserved on one side of each split, second side authored
fresh).

| Source orphan | Target file (via `git mv`) | Ancestry-preserved? | Fresh-authored sibling |
|---------------|----------------------------|---------------------|------------------------|
| RBSDK-director_knight.adoc | RBSDK-director_invest.adoc | yes | — |
| RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc | RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc | yes | — |
| RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc | RBSDD-director_divest.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc (fresh) |
| RBSSL-sa_list.adoc | RBSDR-director_roster.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc (fresh) |

Acronym suggestions are subject to executor verification against terminal
exclusivity (CLAUDE.md prefix discipline) — RBSDD / RBSRD / RBSDR / RBSRL
are candidate mints; if any collide, executor picks alternates and
documents the choice.

Director-side claims the rename for splits because:
- D < R alphabetically gives a stable convention
- Equivalent choice; only constraint is consistency across the two splits

## Two-stage commit strategy (git-mv ancestry)

Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
robust against wholesale content replacement:

**Stage 1 — pure rename, content unchanged:**
```
git mv RBSDK-director_knight.adoc RBSDK-director_invest.adoc
git mv RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc
git mv RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc RBSDD-director_divest.adoc
git mv RBSSL-sa_list.adoc RBSDR-director_roster.adoc
```
Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace
each new path back through the rename to the orphan's full history.

**Stage 2 — content rewrite per-file:**
Each renamed file gets its content replaced to match the new
behavioral contract (see "Behavioral-contract deltas" below). Per-file
commits keep diffs reviewable. Content replacement is wholesale — old
knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics fully gone — but the file path
through which history is traced remains intact.

**Stage 3 — author fresh siblings:**
RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc are written
new. Content shape mirrors the director-side sibling (which got the
ancestry), with role-specific adjustments. These two have no git
ancestry by necessity — one source can't ancestry-bear two targets.

## Behavioral-contract deltas (from RBS0:1045-1119)

Each new spec must reflect these BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts, which differ
from the orphans' originals:

**invest** (was knight/charter):
- Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator
- SA name derived from role + depot moniker (regime-kindled), not operator-supplied
- Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively;
  the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires
- Fatal if SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key

**divest** (was sa_forfeit, now per-role):
- Zero-arg by-role lookup: reads role's installed RBRA, extracts SA email,
  issues REST DELETE
- After successful cloud DELETE, removes role's production RBRA file to
  mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse

**roster** (was sa_list, now per-role):
- Read-only observation
- Emits one fact file per discovered SA via BUS0 multi-fact convention
  (`buf_write_fact_multi` against a registered extension constant); fact
  content is empty, presence-as-fact
- Stdout-substring assertions retire from consumer-facing surface

## Procedural detail to preserve (from orphans)

Each new spec carries forward the orphans' shape:

- `//axhob_operation` opener with operation quoin
- NOTE block describing the operation's role and scope
- `//axhopa_arg_parameter` / `//axhopt_typed_parameter` blocks (or omit
  parameters where the new contract is zero-arg)
- `//axhos_step` blocks with `{rbbc_*}` action verbs (`{rbbc_call}`,
  `{rbbc_require}`, `{rbbc_store}`, `{rbbc_fatal}`, `{rbbc_show}`)
- API endpoint URLs (link form: `link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/...[...]`)
- HTTP response code expectations (200 / 404 handling, etc.)
- `//axhoot_typed_output` block declaring output type
- `//axhoc_completion` closing prose

Match the depth and shape of RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc as the gold standard
for a procedural sub-spec.

## RBS0 restructure

Once the six new sub-spec files exist:

- Anchors `[[rbtgo_director_invest]]` etc. move from RBS0 inline content
  (currently lines 1045-1119) into the new sub-spec files (each file's
  `axhob_operation` opener carries the anchor).
- RBS0 lines 1045-1119 are replaced by `include::RBSDK-director_invest.adoc[]`
  / etc. directives, plus any cross-cutting prose that was inline (the
  preamble at 1039-1043 about cult-verb triad and colophon shape may stay
  inline as cross-cutting framing).
- Mapping section unchanged (the six attribute references already exist
  at RBS0:117-132).

## Orphan disposal

Replaced by ancestry-preserving rename strategy above. No `git rm`
required — the orphans transform into the new files via `git mv` (four
of the six) plus two fresh siblings. CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings
section updates to reflect:
- RBSDK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
- RBSRK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
- RBSSF and RBSSL entries removed (renamed away)
- New entries added for RBSDD, RBSRD, RBSDR, RBSRL (or alternates if
  executor mints differently)

## Companion sweep: RBSRR axho_* prose discipline

RBSRR's three operations paragraphs (`axhro_kindle` / `axhro_validate` /
`axhro_render`) currently leak BUK helper names (`buv_*`, `ZRBRR_*`)
into prose — surfaced during BBAAY notch `fc815a27` review (the followup
landed on top of the original docket-scoped `07730eba` and over-corrected
stale field references into bash-routine references). Sweep with the same
concept-language + linked-term discipline being applied to the new
procedural specs above. Field references via linked terms
(`{rbrr_vessel_dir}` etc.), not bare uppercase. The 30-char GCP project
ID joint-length cap and the operator-visible section-name list
enumerated by `axhro_render` (Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local
Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration,
Secrets Directory, Public Docs) stay — they describe operator-visible
output, not implementation.

## Verification

- Six new sub-spec files exist with full procedural detail (axho* blocks,
  API endpoints, HTTP codes).
- `git log --follow` on each of the four ancestry-preserved files shows
  history reaching back through the rename to the orphan's full log.
- RBS0 includes all six via `include::` directives.
- AsciiDoc rendering of RBS0 shows the new operations at the same depth
  tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM.
- `grep -n 'rbtgo_director_invest\|rbtgo_director_divest\|rbtgo_director_roster\|rbtgo_retriever_invest\|rbtgo_retriever_divest\|rbtgo_retriever_roster'`
  shows anchors live in sub-spec files; mapping references in RBS0; xref
  consumers (RBSGS post-BBAAV) resolve correctly.
- `grep -E 'buv_|ZRBRR_' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc`
  returns zero hits (companion-sweep success).
- CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings section updated.

## Out of scope

- Code changes to rbgg_Governor.sh, rbgi_IAM.sh, or any bash module
- Behavioral changes to operations beyond the BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts
- Changes to other operations' specs (RBSDE, RBSDU, RBSGM, etc.)
- Tabtarget files
- Test fixtures

## References

- BBAAQ commit b73ae194 (wrap) — Surface 2 commit 202c7726 inlined the
  abstracts; orphan disposal explicitly framed as deferred to BBAAN.
- BBAAV commit 021aff0f — companion mechanical xref sweep; surfaced the
  RBS0:1045-1108 inline content for operator review.
- BBAAY notch fc815a27 — RBSRR axho_* paragraphs landed bash-routine
  leakage that the companion sweep above repairs.
- RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc — gold-standard procedural sub-spec shape.
- Orphan files RBSDK / RBSRK / RBSSF / RBSSL — still on disk un-deleted;
  the inputs to the rename strategy.

content.text:
## Mounted: ₣BB → ₢BBAAZ

**Heat:** rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (₣BB) [racing] — 9/21 done

**Paddock takeaways relevant to this pace:**
- Heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine; this pace is a spec-restoration sidequest under the heat's umbrella.
- Cult-verb naming is locked: SA domain uses **invest / divest / roster** (not knight/charter/forfeit/list/muster). Specs must reflect this vocabulary.
- BBAAY just landed RBSRR depot-identity-collapse and over-corrected `axhro_*` prose into bash-routine leakage (notch `fc815a27`) — that's the companion sweep on this pace.

**Pace:** spec-restore-cult-verb-procedural-specs (₢BBAAZ) — silks fits docket cleanly, no rename needed.

**Recent-work context:** Last 4 commits are BBAAN cult-verb-foundation (RBYC fragments, RBCC tinder enrichment, BCG tinder-on-tinder rule). BBAAY closed before that. So the implementation side is moving forward; this pace plugs the spec-side gap left by BBAAQ Surface 2.

**Proposed approach:**
1. **Mint verification first** — check terminal exclusivity for candidate acronyms RBSDD / RBSRD / RBSDR / RBSRL against existing `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS*` tree before committing to them.
2. **Stage 1 single commit** — four `git mv` operations (knight→invest pair, sa_forfeit→director_divest, sa_list→director_roster) with content unchanged, so git rename detection holds.
3. **Stage 2 four per-file rewrites** — wholesale content replacement matching BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts (zero-arg, role-derived names, fact-file emission for roster). Use `RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc` as gold-standard shape. One commit per file for reviewable diffs.
4. **Stage 3** — author two fresh siblings (RBSRD retriever_divest, RBSRL retriever_roster); RBS0 restructure (lines 1045-1119 → include directives); RBSRR companion sweep (`axhro_*` prose: linked terms, drop `buv_*`/`ZRBRR_*` leakage); CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings update.
5. **Verification** — `git log --follow` on each ancestry-preserved file; grep checks for anchor placement and `buv_|ZRBRR_` zero-hit on RBSRR.

**Execution strategy:** Sequential, opus tier. Pure .adoc but heavy judgment per file — each behavioral-contract adaptation requires understanding the orphan's shape, the new abstract from RBS0, and the gold-standard procedural spec depth. Not parallelizable — Stage 1 must precede Stage 2, and the fresh siblings (Stage 3) want the director-side rewrites as templates. Probably 7-9 commits total.

Ready to proceed? Or would you like to adjust scope first?

message.content: I want you to alter the plan to use agents to do the work in parallel.  Use sonnet if you like but one agent per file.  Lately your planning tool is really resisting using parallel agents and that is costing me a lot of time.  Do you want this plan to be plain chat text?

content.text:
Yes, plain chat text is right — this is a tactical execution adjustment, not a docket-shape change. The strategic structure (3 stages, ancestry preservation, contract deltas, verification) all stays as the docket has it; only the dispatch shape changes. No reason to redocket.

## Revised plan — parallel agent dispatch

**Phase A — Sequential setup (main thread, fast):**
1. Mint check: verify candidate acronyms RBSDD / RBSRD / RBSDR / RBSRL against `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/` for terminal exclusivity. If any collide, pick alternates before dispatch.
2. Stage 1 single commit: 4× `git mv` (knight→invest pair + sa_forfeit→director_divest + sa_list→director_roster). `jjx_record` with all four paths.

**Phase B — 6 sonnet agents in parallel, single message:**

Each agent gets the same shared brief: BBAAQ Surface 2 contract excerpt from RBS0:1045-1119, the RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc gold-standard shape, the orphan source content (for procedural detail to preserve — axho* blocks, API URLs, HTTP codes), and its specific role+verb pair. Returns the file authored.

| Agent | Target | Source |
|-------|--------|--------|
| 1 | RBSDK-director_invest.adoc | renamed from RBSDK-director_knight.adoc |
| 2 | RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc | renamed from RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc |
| 3 | RBSDD-director_divest.adoc | renamed from RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc |
| 4 | RBSDR-director_roster.adoc | renamed from RBSSL-sa_list.adoc |
| 5 | RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc | fresh (mirrors director-side contract) |
| 6 | RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc | fresh (mirrors director-side contract) |

After all return: main thread commits per-file (6 commits, reviewable diffs preserved per docket).

**Phase C — 2 agents in parallel:**
- Agent 7 (sonnet): RBS0 restructure — replace lines 1045-1119 with `include::` directives for the six new files; preserve preamble at 1039-1043.
- Agent 8 (sonnet): RBSRR companion sweep — rewrite `axhro_kindle / axhro_validate / axhro_render` prose to use linked terms only, drop `buv_*` / `ZRBRR_*` leakage, retain operator-visible section names.

Main thread handles CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings update inline (mechanical, ~8 lines).

**Phase D — Verification + wrap (main thread):**
- `git log --follow` spot-check on each of the 4 ancestry-preserved files.
- Anchor grep for `rbtgo_director_invest` etc.
- `grep -E 'buv_|ZRBRR_' RBSRR` — zero hits.
- Commit RBS0 + RBSRR + CLAUDE.md (separate commits).

**Total commits:** Stage-1 rename (1) + per-file rewrites (6) + RBS0 (1) + RBSRR (1) + CLAUDE.md (1) = **10 commits**. Authoring work compressed into two parallel waves.

Proceed?

message.content: please try this with multiple agents where reasonable.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_knight}
3	
4	The
5	{rbtr_governor}
6	knights a
7	{rbtr_director}
8	{giam_service_account}
9	with build management permissions:
10	
11	//axhopt_typed_parameter
12	{rbtr_director}
13	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
14	.. {rbbc_require}
15	`«INPUT_INSTANCE»` provided as first argument
16	.. {rbbc_store}
17	`«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»` as `director-«INPUT_INSTANCE»`
18	.. {rbbc_store}
19	`«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` as `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»@{rbrr_depot_project_id}.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
20	
21	//axhoq_precondition
22	* {rbbc_require}
23	`BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` environment variable set and directory exists
24	
25	//axhos_step
26	. *Authenticate as
27	{rbtr_governor}*:
28	.. Execute
29	{rbtoe_governor_authenticate}
30	.. {rbbc_store}
31	`«GOVERNOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
32	
33	//axhos_step
34	. *Create
35	{rbtr_director}
36	Service Account*:
37	.. {rbbc_call}
38	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
39	... accountId: `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»`
40	... displayName: `Recipe Bottle Director («INPUT_INSTANCE»)`
41	... description: `Create/destroy container images for «INPUT_INSTANCE»`
42	... project:
43	{rbrr_depot_project_id}
44	.. {rbbc_require}
45	HTTP 200 response
46	.. {rbbc_store}
47	`«DIRECTOR_UNIQUE_ID»` from response `.uniqueId` field
48	
49	//axhos_step
50	. *Verify Service Account Propagation*:
51	.. {rbbc_call}
52	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
53	.. {rbbc_poll}
54	with backoff until HTTP 200 (newly-created SA may not be immediately resolvable by email)
55	.. NOTE: Poll must use the email address, not the numeric ID. The email-based lookup path has independent propagation from the UID-based path, and subsequent steps (keys.list, keys.create) all use the email address. Polling by UID can succeed while the email path still returns 404.
56	.. {rbbc_require}
57	service account accessible by email
58	
59	//axhos_step
60	. *Preflight: Ensure Pristine Key State*:
61	.. {rbbc_call}
62	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/list[serviceAccounts.keys.list] for `«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
63	.. {rbbc_store}
64	`«EXISTING_KEYS»` from response `.keys[]`
65	.. {rbbc_require}
66	no keys with `keyType: USER_MANAGED` exist
67	.. {rbbc_fatal}
68	if USER_MANAGED keys found: "Manual cleanup required via Console"
69	
70	//axhos_step
71	. *Generate Service Account Key*:
72	.. {rbbc_call}
73	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/create[serviceAccounts.keys.create] for `«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with:
74	... privateKeyType: `TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE`
75	.. Retry up to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX` times on HTTP 404 with `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC` delay between attempts
76	.. NOTE: SA read operations (GET by email, keys.list) may succeed while keys.create POST returns 404 "does not exist" due to write-path propagation delay. Google link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[documents]: "you might need to wait 60 seconds or more before you use the service account." The 404 confirms no key was created, making retry safe. See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
77	.. {rbbc_store}
78	`«DIRECTOR_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` from response `.privateKeyData` field
79	.. {rbtoe_rbra_generate}
80	converts `«DIRECTOR_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` to `«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»`
81	
82	//axhos_step
83	. *Get Depot Project Number*:
84	.. {rbbc_call}
85	link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/get[projects.get] for
86	{rbrr_depot_project_id}
87	.. {rbbc_store}
88	`«PROJECT_NUMBER»` from `.projectNumber` field
89	
90	//axhos_step
91	. *Grant Project-Level Permissions*:
92	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} for
93	{rbrr_depot_project_id}
94	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with roles:
95	... `roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor` (submit and manage builds)
96	... `roles/viewer` (read project resources)
97	... `roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser` (use private pool for vouch `builds.create`)
98	.. Policy version: 3
99	.. {rbbc_require}
100	IAM policy update successful
101	
102	//axhos_step
103	. *Grant
104	{rbtr_mason}
105	Impersonation*:
106	.. NOTE:
107	{rbtr_director}
108	submits builds that execute as
109	{rbtr_mason}.
110	This requires `serviceAccountUser` permission on
111	{rbtr_mason}.
112	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} on
113	{rbtr_mason}
114	service account
115	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser`
116	.. {rbbc_require}
117	IAM binding successful
118	
119	//axhos_step
120	. *Grant
121	{rbtgi_build_bucket}
122	Access*:
123	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket} on
124	{rbtgi_build_bucket}
125	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with roles:
126	... `roles/storage.objectCreator` (upload sources)
127	... `roles/storage.objectViewer` (verify uploads)
128	.. {rbbc_require}
129	bucket IAM update successful
130	
131	//axhos_step
132	. *Grant
133	{rbtgi_repository}
134	Admin Access*:
135	.. {rbbc_call}
136	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/getIamPolicy[repositories.getIamPolicy] via HTTP GET with `options.requestedPolicyVersion=3` query parameter
137	.. NOTE: {gar_registry} proto specifies GET for `getIamPolicy` with no `additional_bindings` for POST. Using POST fails intermittently with HTTP 404 (transcoding layer rejection, not missing repository). See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
138	.. {rbbc_require}
139	HTTP 200 — 404 means repository does not exist (real error, not empty policy)
140	.. {rbbc_call}
141	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/setIamPolicy[repositories.setIamPolicy] for
142	{rbtgi_repository}
143	.. *Complete policy write*: Add ALL expected bindings to the read policy, regardless of what the read returned:
144	... `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin` — Director manages images
145	... `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.writer` — Mason pushes images at build time
146	.. NOTE: Same complete-policy-write pattern as the secret IAM step. Prevents stale read from overwriting Mason's writer binding set by {rbtgo_depot_levy}.
147	.. {rbbc_require}
148	repository IAM set with all expected bindings
149	
150	//axhoot_typed_output
151	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
152	. *Display Installation Instructions*:
153	.. {rbbc_show}
154	Director created successfully
155	.. {rbbc_show}
156	RBRA file location: `«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»`
157	.. {rbbc_show}
158	installation command:
159	... `cp "«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»" "{rbrr_director_rbra_file}"`
160	.. {rbbc_warn}
161	if
162	{rbrr_director_rbra_file}
163	already exists
164	
165	//axhoc_completion
166	Successful execution knights director service account.
167

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_sa_forfeit}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation forfeits a service account from the
5	{rbtgi_depot_project}.
6	It is a multi-role operation that can be invoked by any authenticated role with IAM admin access.
7	
8	//axhopa_arg_parameter
9	{rbgt_service_account}
10	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
11	.. {rbbc_require}
12	`«INPUT_SA_EMAIL»` provided as first argument
13	.. {rbbc_fatal}
14	if missing: "Service account email required"
15	
16	//axhos_step
17	. *Authenticate*:
18	.. {rbbc_require}
19	caller has valid OAuth token with `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform` scope
20	.. {rbbc_store}
21	`«ACCESS_TOKEN»` from caller context
22	
23	//axhos_step
24	. *Forfeit Service Account*:
25	.. {rbbc_call}
26	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/delete[iam.projects.serviceAccounts.delete] with:
27	... method: `DELETE`
28	... endpoint: `https://iam.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{rbrr_depot_project_id}/serviceAccounts/«INPUT_SA_EMAIL»`
29	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «ACCESS_TOKEN»`
30	.. {rbbc_require}
31	HTTP 200 response OR HTTP 404 (already forfeit)
32	.. {rbbc_warn}
33	if HTTP 404: "Service account not found (already forfeit)"
34	
35	//axhoot_typed_output
36	{rbtgi_depot_project}
37	. *Display Results*:
38	.. {rbbc_show}
39	forfeit operation completed
40	
41	//axhoc_completion
42	Successful completion is indicated by the "forfeit operation completed" message.
43

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_sa_list}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation lists all service accounts in the
5	{rbtgi_depot_project}.
6	It is a multi-role operation that can be invoked by any authenticated role with project read access.
7	
8	//axhos_step
9	. *Authenticate*:
10	.. {rbbc_require}
11	caller has valid OAuth token with `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform` scope
12	.. {rbbc_store}
13	`«ACCESS_TOKEN»` from caller context
14	
15	//axhos_step
16	. *List Service Accounts*:
17	.. {rbbc_call}
18	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/list[iam.projects.serviceAccounts.list] with:
19	... method: `GET`
20	... endpoint: `https://iam.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{rbrr_depot_project_id}/serviceAccounts`
21	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «ACCESS_TOKEN»`
22	.. {rbbc_require}
23	HTTP 200 response
24	.. {rbbc_store}
25	`«ACCOUNTS»` from response `.accounts[]` array
26	.. {rbbc_store}
27	`«ACCOUNT_COUNT»` from response `.accounts | length`
28	
29	//axhos_step
30	. *Handle Empty Result*:
31	.. If `«ACCOUNT_COUNT»` is 0:
32	... {rbbc_show}
33	"No service accounts found in project"
34	... Return success
35	
36	//axhoot_typed_output
37	{rbtgi_depot_project}
38	. *Display Results*:
39	.. {rbbc_show}
40	"Found «ACCOUNT_COUNT»` service account(s):"
41	.. For each account in `«ACCOUNTS»`:
42	... {rbbc_show}
43	account `.email` and `.displayName`
44	.. {rbbc_show}
45	listing completed successfully
46	
47	//axhoc_completion
48	Successful execution lists all service accounts in the depot project.
49

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_depot_levy}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation creates an
5	{rbtgi_depot}
6	- a cohesive unit of Recipe Bottle infrastructure including the depot project, build bucket, container repository, Mason service account, and private worker pool
7	that share their productive lifecycle.
8	
9	The
10	{rbtr_payor}
11	creates a new depot infrastructure from the
12	{rbtgi_payor_project}.
13	The operation accepts no CLI parameters; depot identity is read from the kindled regime as `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`.
14	
15	//axhos_step
16	. *Authenticate as
17	{rbtr_payor}*:
18	.. Execute
19	{rbtoe_payor_authenticate}
20	
21	//axhos_step
22	. *Validate region against Artifact Registry locations*:
23	.. {rbbc_call}
24	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations/list[locations.list] for the authenticated project
25	.. {rbbc_require}
26	`{rbrr_gcp_region}` present in the returned location set
27	
28	//axhos_step
29	. *Derive Depot Identity*:
30	.. {rbbc_store}
31	`«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (resolved by `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`)
32	.. {rbbc_store}
33	`«GAR_REPOSITORY»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}-gar` (resolved by `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY`)
34	.. {rbbc_store}
35	`«WORKER_POOL_STEM»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}-pool` (resolved by `RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`)
36	.. {rbbc_store}
37	`«BUILD_BUCKET»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (resolved by `RBDC_GCS_BUCKET`)
38	.. NOTE: Joint length `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}` + `d-` + `${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` ≤ 30 chars (GCP project ID limit) is enforced upstream by `zrbrr_enforce` at regime kindle time, so this step performs no length validation.
39	
40	//axhos_step
41	. *Create Depot Project*:
42	.. {rbbc_submit}
43	link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/create[projects.create] with:
44	... projectId: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
45	... displayName: `RB Depot: «RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER»`
46	... parent: `{rbrp_parent_type}s/{rbrp_parent_id}` (if parent_type != `none`)
47	.. {rbbc_await}
48	returned operation
49	.. {rbbc_require}
50	project creation successful
51	.. NOTE: Project name collision against the GCP soft-delete graveyard surfaces here as fatal — re-using a moniker after `{rbtgo_depot_unmake}` requires waiting out the 30-day retention window. The operator-visible "fails if exists" property is intentional under the (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) shape.
52	
53	//axhos_step
54	. *Link Billing Account*:
55	.. {rbbc_call}
56	link:https://cloud.google.com/billing/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects/updateBillingInfo[cloudbilling.projects.updateBillingInfo] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
57	.. Set billingAccountName: `billingAccounts/{rbrp_billing_account_id}`
58	.. {rbbc_require}
59	HTTP 200 response confirming billing linkage
60	
61	//axhos_step
62	. *Get Depot Project Number*:
63	.. {rbbc_call}
64	link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/get[projects.get] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
65	.. {rbbc_store}
66	`«PROJECT_NUMBER»` from `.name` field (CRM v3 returns `projects/{number}`)
67	.. {rbbc_require}
68	valid numeric project number after extracting from name
69	
70	//axhos_step
71	. *Enable Depot Project APIs*:
72	.. For each service in [`artifactregistry`, `cloudbuild`, `containeranalysis`, `iam`, `serviceusage`, `storage`]:
73	... Execute
74	{rbtoe_api_enable}
75	with service name and `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
76	.. {rbbc_require}
77	all APIs enabled successfully
78	
79	//axhos_step
80	. *Create Private Worker Pool*:
81	.. {rbbc_call}
82	link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.workerPools/create[workerPools.create] with:
83	... parent: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/{rbrr_gcp_region}`
84	... workerPoolId: `«WORKER_POOL_STEM»`
85	... body:
86	+
87	----
88	{
89	  "privatePoolV1Config": {
90	    "workerConfig": {
91	      "machineType": "«RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE»"
92	    }
93	  }
94	}
95	----
96	.. NOTE:
97	{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}
98	is a Compute Engine machine type (e.g., `e2-standard-2`), consumed at pool creation time.
99	Pool scales to zero workers when idle — no idle cost accrues.
100	.. Treat HTTP 409 CONFLICT as success (pool already exists — idempotent)
101	
102	//axhos_step
103	. *Verify IAM Propagation*:
104	.. NOTE: After API enablement, IAM permissions may not be immediately available due to eventual consistency.
105	.. {rbbc_poll}
106	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/list[artifactregistry.repositories.list] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` in `{rbrr_gcp_region}`
107	.. Poll at `RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC` intervals until HTTP 200 or `RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC` timeout
108	.. {rbbc_require}
109	HTTP 200 response confirming IAM propagation complete
110	.. {rbbc_fatal}
111	if timeout: "IAM propagation timeout - artifactregistry API not accessible"
112	
113	//axhos_step
114	. *Create Build Bucket*:
115	.. {rbbc_call}
116	link:https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/insert[storage.buckets.insert] with:
117	... name: `«BUILD_BUCKET»`
118	... location: `{rbrr_gcp_region}`
119	... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
120	... lifecycle rule: delete objects after 1 day
121	
122	//axhos_step
123	. *Create Container Repository*:
124	.. {rbbc_submit}
125	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/create[artifactregistry.repositories.create] with:
126	... repositoryId: `«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
127	... format: `DOCKER`
128	... location: `{rbrr_gcp_region}`
129	... parent: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/{rbrr_gcp_region}`
130	.. {rbbc_await}
131	returned operation
132	
133	//axhos_step
134	. *Create
135	{rbtr_mason}
136	Service Account*:
137	.. NOTE: The
138	{rbtr_mason}
139	replaces Cloud Build's implicit service account to provide deterministic permissions and predictable authorization within the depot infrastructure.
140	.. {rbbc_call}
141	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
142	... accountId: `mason-«RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER»`
143	... displayName: `Mason for RB Depot: «RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER»`
144	... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
145	.. {rbbc_store}
146	`«MASON_EMAIL»` from response `.email` field
147	
148	//axhos_step
149	. *Configure
150	{rbtr_mason}
151	Permissions*:
152	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_repo} on `«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
153	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.writer`
154	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket} on `«BUILD_BUCKET»`
155	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/storage.objectViewer`
156	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} on `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
157	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/viewer`
158	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/logging.logWriter`
159	
160	//axhos_step
161	. *Enable Cloud Build service agent to impersonate
162	{rbtr_mason}*:
163	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} on `«MASON_EMAIL»`
164	.. Add member: `serviceAccount:service-«PROJECT_NUMBER»@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
165	.. Grant role: `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator`
166	.. NOTE: Cloud Build service agent is Google-provisioned (auto-created when Cloud Build API is enabled), not a user-created service account. The `tokenCreator` role enables build steps running as {rbtr_mason} (via the build JSON's `serviceAccount` field) to generate fresh access tokens for GCP API calls — required for GAR authentication (docker login, crane push). This is distinct from `serviceAccountUser` (actAs), which the {rbtr_director} holds on {rbtr_mason} for submitting builds.
167	.. {rbbc_require}
168	IAM binding successful
169	
170	//axhoot_typed_output
171	{rbtgi_depot}
172	. *Confirm Depot Creation*:
173	.. {rbbc_show}
174	single-line success banner naming `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` and the
175	{rbtgo_governor_mantle}
176	follow-up hint
177	
178	//axhoot_typed_output
179	{rbtgi_depot}
180	. *Update Depot Tracking*:
181	.. Execute
182	{rbtoe_depot_list_update}
183	to refresh per-moniker depot fact files
184	
185	//axhoc_completion
186	Successful execution creates depot infrastructure with configured build bucket, container repository, Mason service account, and private worker pool — all derived from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`.
187	Proceed to
188	{rbtgo_governor_mantle}
189	to create the
190	{rbtr_governor}
191	for this depot.
192

lastPrompt: please try this with multiple agents where reasonable.

content.text:
Mint check clean — RBSDD, RBSDR, RBSRD, RBSRL not present. All four candidates available.

Loading task tracking and reading RBS0 contracts.

content.content:
1030	{rbtr_governor}
1031	configuration
1032	({rbtgog_governor}).
1033	
1034	include::RBSGM-governor_mantle.adoc[]
1035	
1036	== {rbtr_governor} Operations
1037	
1038	The
1039	{rbtr_retriever}
1040	and
1041	{rbtr_director}
1042	service accounts share a cult-verb triad: invest mints the SA and emits its RBRA credential bytes, divest tears it down, and roster lists existing instances.
1043	Each verb has per-role instances; the colophon shape is `rbw-a<role-letter><verb-letter>` where the letter case carries semantic load — capital `I` (invest) and `D` (divest) for cloud-mutating verbs, lowercase `r` (roster) for read-only observation, mirroring the existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent.
1044	{rbtgo_governor_mantle} stays as the singleton-per-depot exception; governor SA cleanup folds into {rbtgo_depot_unmake}.
1045	
1046	[[rbtgo_director_divest]]
1047	//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
1048	=== {rbtgo_director_divest}
1049	
1050	Divests a
1051	{rbtr_director}
1052	service account
1053	({rbtgog_governor}).
1054	
1055	Zero-arg: reads the role's installed RBRA, extracts the SA email, and issues a REST DELETE.
1056	After successful cloud DELETE, removes the role's production RBRA file to mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse.
1057	
1058	[[rbtgo_director_invest]]
1059	//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
1060	=== {rbtgo_director_invest}
1061	
1062	Invests a
1063	{rbtr_director}
1064	service account
1065	({rbtgog_governor}).
1066

include::RBSGM-governor_mantle.adoc[]

== {rbtr_governor} Operations

The
{rbtr_retriever}
and
{rbtr_director}
service accounts share a cult-verb triad: invest mints the SA and emits its RBRA credential bytes, divest tears it down, and roster lists existing instances.
Each verb has per-role instances; the colophon shape is `rbw-a<role-letter><verb-letter>` where the letter case carries semantic load — capital `I` (invest) and `D` (divest) for cloud-mutating verbs, lowercase `r` (roster) for read-only observation, mirroring the existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent.
{rbtgo_governor_mantle} stays as the singleton-per-depot exception; governor SA cleanup folds into {rbtgo_depot_unmake}.

[[rbtgo_director_divest]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_director_divest}

Divests a
{rbtr_director}
service account
({rbtgog_governor}).

Zero-arg: reads the role's installed RBRA, extracts the SA email, and issues a REST DELETE.
After successful cloud DELETE, removes the role's production RBRA file to mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse.

[[rbtgo_director_invest]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_director_invest}

Invests a
{rbtr_director}
service account
({rbtgog_governor}).

Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator; SA name derived from role and depot moniker (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).
Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively (the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires).
Fatal if the SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key.

[[rbtgo_director_roster]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_director_roster}

Rosters
{rbtr_director}
service accounts
({rbtgog_governor}).

Read-only observation.
Emits one fact file per discovered SA via the BUS0 multi-element fact-file convention (`buf_write_fact_multi` against a registered extension constant); fact content is empty, presence-as-fact.
Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

[[rbtgo_retriever_divest]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_retriever_divest}

Divests a
{rbtr_retriever}
service account
({rbtgog_governor}).

Zero-arg: reads the role's installed RBRA, extracts the SA email, and issues a REST DELETE.
After successful cloud DELETE, removes the role's production RBRA file to mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse.

[[rbtgo_retriever_invest]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped

Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator; SA name derived from role and depot moniker (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).
Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively (the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires).
Fatal if the SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key.

[[rbtgo_retriever_roster]]

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_vouch}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation vouches for every *pending*
5	{rbtga_ark}
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository},
8	where *pending* means a hallmark whose subtree contains both
9	{rbtga_ark_image}
10	and
11	{rbtga_ark_about}
12	basenames but no
13	{rbtga_ark_vouch}.
14	The
15	{rbtr_director}
16	enumerates the full hallmark corpus via a single GAR REST `packages.list` call (the same shared helper used by
17	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally}),
18	identifies pending hallmarks, resolves the vessel name from each hallmark's
19	{rbtga_ark_about}
20	metadata (since vessel identity no longer lives in the GAR path), and dispatches
21	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
22	per hallmark.
23	Mode-awareness (conjure, bind, graft) lives inside
24	{rbtgo_ark_vouch};
25	batch vouch does not branch on mode.
26	
27	NOTE: *Fail-fast semantics.* If
28	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
29	fails for any hallmark, the batch terminates via fatal error at that hallmark.
30	There is no skip-with-warning path and no summary-count-masking of failures: a broken hallmark demands immediate attention, not quiet continuation.
31	
32	The
33	{rbtr_director}
34	batch-vouches arks:
35	
36	//axhos_step
37	. *Authenticate as
38	{rbtr_director}*:
39	.. Execute
40	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
41	.. {rbbc_store}
42	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
43	
44	//axhos_step
45	. *Enumerate Hallmark Packages*:
46	.. {rbbc_call}
47	the shared hallmark enumeration helper — a single
48	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list]
49	call under `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/` (`RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT`), filtered to `<hallmark>/<basename>` shape
50	.. {rbbc_store}
51	`«PAIRS»` as sorted `<hallmark> <basename>` pairs (same shape produced for
52	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally})
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Identify Pending Hallmarks (Pass 1)*:
56	.. Iterate `«PAIRS»` in sort order; each contiguous run of the same hallmark is one
57	{rbtga_hallmark}'s
58	ark inventory.
59	.. For each hallmark, record whether
60	{rbtga_ark_image},
61	{rbtga_ark_about},
62	and
63	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
64	each appear in its run.
65	.. A hallmark is *pending* when `image` and `about` are present and `vouch` is absent.
66	Collect pending hallmarks into `«PENDING»` (in-memory sequence).
67	.. If `«PENDING»` is empty, the procedure completes successfully with no vouches submitted.
68	
69	//axhos_step
70	. *Resolve Vessel and Vouch Per Pending Hallmark (Pass 2)*:
71	.. For each `«HALLMARK»` in `«PENDING»`:
72	... Extract the hallmark's
73	{rbtga_ark_about}
74	artifact (package `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/«HALLMARK»/about`, tag `«HALLMARK»`) to a scratch directory via the shared GAR artifact extraction helper
75	... Read `.vessel_name` from the extracted `build_info.json` via `jq`
76	... {rbbc_store}
77	`«VESSEL_DIR»` as `«RBRR_VESSEL_DIR»/«VESSEL_NAME»`
78	... {rbbc_require}
79	`«VESSEL_DIR»` exists on disk — the vessel named in `about` metadata must correspond to a configured vessel in the current regime
80	... {rbbc_fatal}
81	if the `about` artifact cannot be extracted, `build_info.json` is missing, `.vessel_name` is empty, or `«VESSEL_DIR»` does not exist — the `about` artifact is the sole source of vessel identity for this batch; a gap there is a fail-fast stop
82	... Invoke
83	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
84	with `«VESSEL_DIR»` and `«HALLMARK»`.
85	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
86	loads the vessel's
87	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
88	internally and dispatches the appropriate verification (conjure: SLSA DSSE verification; bind: digest-pin; graft: GRAFTED stamp).
89	... {rbbc_fatal}
90	if
91	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
92	fails — the batch terminates at the broken hallmark.
93	
94	//axhoot_typed_output
95	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
96	. *Display Summary*:
97	.. {rbbc_show}
98	`Batch vouch complete — N/N hallmark(s) vouched` where N is the initial pending count
99	
100	//axhoc_completion
101	Successful execution creates one
102	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
103	artifact per pending hallmark (N total, where N = initial pending count).
104	The batch runs sequentially; a failure at any hallmark halts the remainder.
105	+
106	*Scope of vessel resolution*: Reading `vessel_name` from `build_info.json` is a narrow anticipation of the broader vessel-from-metadata pattern (AAL territory) — applied locally inside batch vouch only, because
107	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
108	's signature still takes a vessel directory.
109	Other operations (tally, plumb, abjure) continue to not surface vessel.
110	+
111	*Two-pass shape*: Pass 1 collects pending hallmarks into memory before Pass 2 begins processing.
112	Each
113	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
114	invocation sources credentials and issues Cloud Build calls that would silently consume loop stdin if the pending set were streamed; the in-memory collection decouples enumeration from dispatch.
115	+
116	*Idempotency*: Re-running batch vouch after a successful batch finds an empty pending set (every prior pending hallmark now has `vouch` present) and exits with no action.
117	+
118	*No mode pre-check*: Earlier shapes of this operation pre-probed SLSA provenance for conjure-mode hallmarks to skip-with-warning those lacking provenance.
119	That pattern is gone — all mode-specific verification happens inside
120	{rbtgo_ark_vouch},
121	and any failure there surfaces as a fail-fast stop rather than being absorbed into a summary count.
122

content.text: Four clean renames detected. Committing Stage 1.

params.intent: Stage 1 of cult-verb procedural-spec restoration: pure git mv of four orphan procedural specs to their cult-verb destinations, content unchanged so git rename detection holds for ancestry preservation. RBSDK-director_knight.adoc → RBSDK-director_invest.adoc and RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc → RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc (knight→invest, 1:1 ancestry). RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc → RBSDD-director_divest.adoc and RBSSL-sa_list.adoc → RBSDR-director_roster.adoc (the per-role splits each claim director-side ancestry per docket convention; retriever-side fresh siblings RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc author in Stage 3). Mint candidates RBSDD/RBSDR (this stage) and RBSRD/RBSRL (Stage 3) verified clean against existing RBS* tree — no terminal-exclusivity collision. Stage 2 lands per-file content rewrites against the BBAAQ Surface 2 behavioral contracts now sitting at RBS0:1045-1119 inline; the rewrites are wholesale (singleton-per-role invest, zero-arg by-role divest, fact-multi-emitting roster — fully retiring the knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics) but the rename happens here in isolation so git log --follow can trace each new path through the rename to the orphan's full history. Wholesale rewrites combined with rename in a single commit would risk dropping below git's similarity threshold and losing the rename detection — hence the two-stage discipline.

//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_retriever_divest}

NOTE: This operation divests a {rbtr_retriever} {giam_service_account} from the {rbtgi_depot_project}.
The {rbtr_governor} reads the retriever's installed RBRA, extracts the SA email from the RBRA's `client_email` field, issues a REST DELETE against the IAM API, and removes the production RBRA file from disk to mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse.
No arguments are required; the target SA is derived entirely from the kindled regime and the retriever's installed credential file.

//axhos_step
. *Authenticate as {rbtr_governor}*:
.. Execute
{rbtoe_governor_authenticate}
.. {rbbc_store}
`«GOVERNOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls

. *Read Retriever RBRA and Extract SA Email*:
.. {rbbc_require}
RBRA file present at
{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}
.. {rbbc_fatal}
if file missing: "Retriever RBRA file not found — retriever may not be invested"
.. {rbbc_store}
`«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»` from RBRA's `client_email` field
.. {rbbc_fatal}
if `«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»` is empty or absent: "client_email field missing from retriever RBRA"

//axhos_step
. *Delete Retriever Service Account*:
.. {rbbc_call}
link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/delete[iam.projects.serviceAccounts.delete] with:
... method: `DELETE`
... endpoint: `https://iam.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{rbtgi_depot_project}/serviceAccounts/«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»`
... header: `Authorization: Bearer «GOVERNOR_TOKEN»`
.. {rbbc_require}
HTTP 200 response OR HTTP 404 (already divested)
.. {rbbc_warn}
if HTTP 404: "Retriever service account not found in cloud (already divested)"

//axhos_step
. *Remove Production RBRA File*:
.. Execute
{rbtoe_rbra_remove}
for the retriever's RBRA file at
{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file},
mirroring cloud state and preventing stale-credential reuse
.. {rbbc_require}
file removal successful or file already absent

//axhos_step
. *Display Result*:
.. {rbbc_show}
single-line success notice naming `«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»` and confirming divestiture, or a 404-already-gone notice if cloud state was already clean

//axhoc_completion
Successful execution removes the {rbtr_retriever} {giam_service_account} from the {rbtgi_depot_project} and deletes the production RBRA file, leaving no stale credentials on disk.
Proceed to
{rbtgo_retriever_invest}
to re-issue a fresh retriever SA when needed.

1	## Command Reference (Generated)
2	
3	<!-- Generated by buz_emit_context from zipper registry. Do not edit. -->
4	<!-- Regenerate: tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh -->
5	
6	**Folio** is the runtime target value passed to a command (nameplate moniker, role name, etc.).
7	The Folio column shows how each tabtarget receives it:
8	
9	- **imprint**: Folio is baked into the filename — one tabtarget per target (e.g., `tt/rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh`)
10	- **param1**: Folio is passed as a command-line argument (e.g., `tt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.sh tadmor`)
11	- **—**: No folio needed — standalone command
12	
13	### Accounts — Service account management (`rbw-a`)
14	
15	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Folio | Purpose |
16	|----------|-------------|-------|---------|
17	| `rbw-aM` | PayorMantlesGovernor | — | Create/replace governor service account |
18	| `rbw-arI` | GovernorInvestsRetriever | param1 | Invest a Retriever service account for an identity |
19	| `rbw-adI` | GovernorInvestsDirector | param1 | Invest a Director service account for an identity |
20	| `rbw-arD` | GovernorDivestsRetriever | param1 | Divest a Retriever service account by identity |
21	| `rbw-adD` | GovernorDivestsDirector | param1 | Divest a Director service account by identity |
22	| `rbw-arr` | GovernorRostersRetrievers | — | Roster Retriever service accounts (emit per-identity fact files) |
23	| `rbw-adr` | GovernorRostersDirectors | — | Roster Director service accounts (emit per-identity fact files) |
24	
25	### Crucible — Container runtime (`rbw-c`)
26	
27	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Folio | Purpose |
28	|----------|-------------|-------|---------|
29	| `rbw-cC` | Charge | imprint | Charge crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle containers) |

input.description: RBSRR companion sweep
input.prompt:
You are repairing three operations-prose paragraphs in an AsciiDoc spec file to use linked-term discipline (project's vocabulary convention) instead of leaking BUK helper-function names into spec text.

TARGET FILE:
/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc

CONTEXT:
This is the spec for `rbrr.env` — the repo-level regime configuration. It has three operations paragraphs that describe what the kindle / validate / render operations do at runtime:
- `axhro_kindle` — describes how the regime kindles fields
- `axhro_validate` — describes how the regime validates

content.content:
=== Verification 1: git log --follow on 4 ancestry-preserved files ===

--- RBSDK-director_invest ---
35b8ec88 jjb:1011-92c4951b:₢BBAAZ:n: Stage 2 of cult-verb procedural-spec restoration: rewrite RBSDK-director_invest.adoc against BBAAQ Surface 2 invest contract (RBS0:1058-1069). Wholesale content replacement on top of the Stage-1 git mv (b30c0d0d) — old knight semantics fully retired, but rename detection through that commit preserves git ancestry from the original RBSDK-director_knight.adoc. Deltas: drop axhopt_typed_parameter (zero-arg now — SA name derived from regime); drop BURD_OUTPUT_DIR precondition; add probe-if-exists step right after authentication that fatally errors if the SA already exists, instructing operator to divest first to re-key (singleton-per-role discipline); replace SA-name derivation as ${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} via the regime-kindled depot moniker (no operator-supplied instance); flip endpoint URLs from retired {rbrr_depot_project_id} to {rbtgi_depot_project} linked term (BBAAY retired the bare RBRR field; depot project ID now derives as RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID from the cloud-prefix/moniker tuple); replace final 'Display Installation Instructions' block with assay-slot output discipline (writes RBRA bytes to assay output exclusively, retiring BURD_OUTPUT_DIR credential write); update axhoc_completion to invest semantics. Preserved: all GCP REST API endpoint URLs (iam.serviceAccounts.create/get, serviceAccounts.keys.list/create, projects.get, repositories.getIamPolicy/setIamPolicy), HTTP 200/404 response code expectations, NOTE blocks about email-vs-UID propagation independence and 404-during-create write-path propagation delay, complete-policy-write pattern, cross-reference to <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>, full IAM grant set (project-level cloudbuild.builds.editor + viewer + workerPoolUser, mason serviceAccountUser, build-bucket access, GAR repoAdmin), Policy version: 3 marker, axho* block grammar (axhob_operation, axhos_step, axhoot_typed_output, axhoc_completion). 165 lines, 13 axhos_step blocks, anchor opener //axhob_operation + {rbtgo_director_invest} matching RBSDE/RBSDV gold-standard shape. {rbrr_depot_project_id} grep returns zero hits.
b30c0d0d jjb:1011-4ed6e57c:₢BBAAZ:n: Stage 1 of cult-verb procedural-spec restoration: pure git mv of four orphan procedural specs to their cult-verb destinations, content unchanged so git rename detection holds for ancestry preservation. RBSDK-director_knight.adoc → RBSDK-director_invest.adoc and RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc → RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc (knight→invest, 1:1 ancestry). RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc → RBSDD-director_divest.adoc and RBSSL-sa_list.adoc → RBSDR-director_roster.adoc (the per-role splits each claim director-side ancestry per docket convention; retriever-side fresh siblings RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc author in Stage 3). Mint candidates RBSDD/RBSDR (this stage) and RBSRD/RBSRL (Stage 3) verified clean against existing RBS* tree — no terminal-exclusivity collision. Stage 2 lands per-file content rewrites against the BBAAQ Surface 2 behavioral contracts now sitting at RBS0:1045-1119 inline; the rewrites are wholesale (singleton-per-role invest, zero-arg by-role divest, fact-multi-emitting roster — fully retiring the knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics) but the rename happens here in isolation so git log --follow can trace each new path through the rename to the orphan's full history. Wholesale rewrites combined with rename in a single commit would risk dropping below git's similarity threshold and losing the rename detection — hence the two-stage discipline.
936b312b jjb:1011-c9d3ee5f:₢AUAAt:n: Purge legacy rubric and gitlab dead code: remove zrbgg_rubric_preflight function and call sites, RBGC_RUBRIC constants, RBRA/RBRR rubric enrollment lines, rblm_cli stripping case, elimination comments across 8 .sh files; clean rubric/gitlab references from 7 .adoc specs and README; remove RBSRI mapping from CLAUDE.md and rubric gitignore entry
fd487cff jjb:1011-a426557a:₢AxAAN:n: Rename Payor operations to liturgical verbs: depot_create→depot_levy, depot_destroy→depot_unmake, governor_reset→governor_mantle. Shell functions, zipper constants+colophons, spec quoins (rbtgo_depot_levy, rbtgo_depot_unmake, rbtgo_governor_mantle), subdocs (RBSDE, RBSDU, RBSGM, RBSGD, RBSDN, RBSGS, RBSPI, RBSQB, RBSRI, RBSRR, RBSDK, RBSCIG, RBSCTD), consumer docs, and lexicon examples all updated.
595ed577 jjb:1011-e6313877:₢AxAAM:n: Rename Governor SA operations to liturgical verbs: charter retriever, knight director, forfeit service account
03e722ed jjb:1011-d93343ce::i: OFFICIUM 260331-1007
f40d105b jjb:1011-9bc39230:₢AvAAG:n: Sweep eliminated terms (GitLab, triggers, slsa-verifier, CB v2, rubric repo) from 14 spec sub-documents. RBSDC heavy rewrite (removed 7 GitLab/CBv2 steps). RBSCB Current Posture updated for builds.create + pouch + DSSE. RBSDI/RBSRC preconditions removed. RBSGR connectionViewer eliminated. RBSRG slsa-verifier vars eliminated. RBSAG/RBSGS/RBSQB trigger_build references updated. RBSCTD marked superseded. RBS0 active-content fixes (vouch verdict, concurrency section, step architecture).
d670d895 jjb:1011-f3791927:₢AwAAN:n: Rename operation hierarchy markers to entity voicing convention: axhop_parameter_from_type→axhopt_typed_parameter, axhop_parameter_from_arg→axhopa_arg_parameter, axhoo_output_of_type→axhoot_typed_output. --- RBSRK-retriever_invest ---
3f2e6e4d jjb:1011-35b8ec88:₢BBAAZ:n: Stage 2 of cult-verb procedural-spec restoration: rewrite RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc against BBAAQ Surface 2 invest contract (RBS0:1096-1107). lastPrompt: notch, then review the work done by the agents and make a list of terse repairs/ improvements.

content.text:
Tree was already clean from the 10 jjx_record commits — no notch needed. Reviewing the 6 sub-specs and RBS0 restructure now.

## Repairs / improvements (terse)

**Load-bearing:**

1. **RBSDD wrong actor in opening prose** (line 5-9): `The {rbtr_director} divests the {rbtr_director}...` — divest is governor-authenticated per BBAAQ. Should be `{rbtr_governor}` as subject.

2. **Missing `//axhoot_typed_output` in RBSDK and RBSRK**. Orphans had `//axhoot_typed_output` + `{rbtgi_rbra_file}`; agents replaced with `//axhos_step` "Write Credential to Assay Output Slot". Per RBSDE gold standard, `//axhoot_typed_output` is load-bearing — restore it (likely a new {rbtga_assay_output} or similar quoin if one exists; otherwise inline).

3. **Roster `buf_write_fact_multi` argument shape mis-stated** in both RBSDR (line 50-51) and RBSRL (line 41-43). Signature is `<file_root> <ext> <value>`. RBSRL says `value «IDENTITY»` — that puts identity into file content, but RBS0:1119 mandates empty content. RBSDR says "basename «IDENTITY»" — ambiguous. Restate both as: `file_root = <roster_dir>/<identity>`, `ext = <constant>`, `value = ""`.

**Consistency drift across siblings:**

4. **Opener style split**: RBSDE/RBSDV gold-standard uses `NOTE: This operation …` block. RBSRD and RBSRL follow that. RBSDK / RBSRK / RBSDD / RBSDR use prose-only opens. Convert the 4 to NOTE: blocks.

5. **RBSRK stray `//axhoq_precondition`** (lines 14-16): "regime kindled with valid (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER) pair" — no sibling has this. Drop, or add to all 6.

6. **Endpoint URL form**: RBSDD line 43 uses `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` placeholder + 2-line parenthetical; RBSRD line 32 uses `{rbtgi_depot_project}` inline. Pull RBSDD to RBSRD's form.

7. **Step heading verb drift**: RBSDD step 3 = "Divest Service Account"; RBSRD step 3 = "Delete Retriever Service Account". Pick one (probably "Divest" matches the cult-verb).

8. **Empty-result branch asymmetry**: RBSDR (lines 39-42) emits "No director SAs found" + early return; RBSRL doesn't. Mirror in RBSRL or drop from RBSDR.

9. **Assay-output step shape drift**: RBSDK uses `{rbbc_store}` action verb; RBSRK uses bare prose. Align.

10. **`axhoc_completion` shape drift between RBSDR and RBSRL**. Make parallel.

**Minor:**

11. **Plural attribute mis-use**: `{giam_service_account}s` (English-suffix plural) at RBS0:1074, RBS0:1109, RBSRL:4. Project defines `{giam_service_account_s}` at RBS0:200 — switch.

12. **RBSDD post-DELETE require step** (line 54-55): "{rbbc_require} the preceding DELETE succeeded (HTTP 200 or 404...)" — slightly tautological since the prior step already required 200/404; could fold into the step description rather than restate.

Want me to slate a follow-on pace for these, or fix in this pace before wrap?

message.content: advise me about repairing each of these.  CAn we do this work with parallel agents for speed?

content.text:
## Per-item advice

**1. RBSDD wrong actor.** Trivial — grammar swap. `The {rbtr_director} divests…` → `The {rbtr_governor} divests…`. Folds into the NOTE-block conversion (#4).

**2. Missing `//axhoot_typed_output` in RBSDK/RBSRK.** Orphans declared `{rbtgi_rbra_file}` as the typed output. New contract still emits RBRA-format bytes, just through the assay slot rather than a static path. Restore the `//axhoot_typed_output` + `{rbtgi_rbra_file}` opener for the final block; merge with the assay-slot prose in the step body (collapses with #9).

**3. Roster `buf_write_fact_multi` arg shape.** Signature is `<file_root> <ext> <value>`. Existing depot-fact precedent in `rbgp_Payor.sh` uses `file_root = <dir>/<identity>`, `value = ""` for presence-as-fact. Repair both roster specs to that form: file_root composed as `<roster_subdir>/«IDENTITY»`, ext is the registered constant, value is empty string.

**4. Opener NOTE style.** Mechanical conversion. Reshape each prose opener to `NOTE: This operation <verb>s a {rbtr_role} {giam_service_account} <scope-clause>. <one-line param/derivation note>.` Match RBSDE:4-13 and RBSDV:4-25 cadence.

**5. RBSRK stray `//axhoq_precondition`.** Drop it. The "regime kindled" precondition is universal for all governor-authenticated ops and not called out elsewhere — calling it out here is non-load-bearing.

**6. RBSDD endpoint placeholder.** Replace lines 43-45 (`«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` + 2-line parenthetical) with RBSRD's inline form: `endpoint: \`https://iam.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{rbtgi_depot_project}/serviceAccounts/«SA_EMAIL»\``.

**7. Step heading verb drift.** Cult-verb is "divest"; REST API verb is "delete". Step heading should match the cult-verb level. Standardize both to "Divest <Role> Service Account": RBSDD step 3 → "Divest Director Service Account"; RBSRD step 3 → "Divest Retriever Service Account".

**8. Empty-result early-exit asymmetry.** Drop from RBSDR rather than mirror to RBSRL. The presence-as-fact convention naturally degrades to zero emissions on empty input; the `Display Summary` step at the end already reports the count. The early-exit branch is non-load-bearing scaffolding.

**9. Assay-output step shape.** Pick the `{rbbc_store}` action-verb form (RBSDK's). Or — better — fold this step into #2's `//axhoot_typed_output` block (the assay write IS the typed output declaration).

**10. `axhoc_completion` shape drift.** Pick a parallel template, e.g.: `Successful execution rosters {rbtr_<role>} service accounts via per-SA fact emission — one \`<ext-value>\` fact file per discovered identity. The operation is read-only; no service accounts are created or modified.` Apply to both.

**11. Plural attribute.** Mechanical: 3 sites — RBS0:1074, RBS0:1109, RBSRL:4. `{giam_service_account}s` → `{giam_service_account_s}`.

**12. RBSDD post-DELETE require redundancy.** Minor. The preceding step's `{rbbc_require} HTTP 200 OR 404` already gates entry to step 4. Either drop the duplicate require, or fold into step prose ("After successful DELETE…"). Drop is cleaner.

## Parallelization plan — yes, 7 agents in parallel

One agent per file. File-disjoint, no edit conflicts.

| Agent | File | Repairs |
|-------|------|---------|
| A | RBSDK-director_invest | #2, #4, #9 |
| B | RBSRK-retriever_invest | #2, #4, #5, #9 |
| C | RBSDD-director_divest | #1+#4 (combined), #6, #7, #12 |
| D | RBSRD-retriever_divest | #7 (one step heading swap — could fold into main) |
| E | RBSDR-director_roster | #3, #4, #8, #10 |
| F | RBSRL-retriever_roster | #3, #10, #11 (one site) |
| G | RBS0-SpecTop | #11 (two sites) |

To prevent the cross-file consistency drift that produced these issues in the first place, each agent's prompt locks in **target forms verbatim** (NOTE: block phrasing skeleton, axhoot_typed_output block shape, completion-prose template, buf_write_fact_multi argument-statement template). Agents don't synthesize style — they apply specified shapes.

Agent D's repair is small enough (one heading change) that I could just do it in the main thread and skip dispatching a 7th agent. Same for G (two `{giam_service_account_s}` swaps). Your call:
- **6-agent dispatch + 2 main-thread mechanical edits**, or
- **7-agent dispatch with full uniformity**.

Either way, expect commit count ≈ 6-7 (one per file). Ready to launch on your go.

5	The {rbtr_governor} mints the SA, generates its credential key, and writes the RBRA bytes to the assay output slot.
6	The operation is singleton-per-role: no instance argument; the SA identity is derived from regime constants as `${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
7	Fatal if the SA already exists — operator must {rbtgo_director_divest} first to re-key.
8	
9	//axhos_step
10	. *Authenticate as
11	{rbtr_governor}*:
12	.. Execute
13	{rbtoe_governor_authenticate}
14	.. {rbbc_store}
15	`«GOVERNOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
16	
17	//axhos_step
18	. *Derive
19	{rbtr_director}
20	Service Account Identity*:
21	.. {rbbc_store}
22	`«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»` as `${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (singleton per role per depot, regime-kindled via
23	{rbrr_depot_moniker})
24	.. {rbbc_store}
25	`«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` as `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»@${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com` (depot project ID derived from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`)
26	
27	//axhos_step
28	. *Preflight: Confirm Service Account Does Not Exist*:
29	.. {rbbc_call}
30	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
31	.. {rbbc_fatal}
32	if HTTP 200: service account already exists — operator must execute
33	{rbtgo_director_divest}
34	before re-investing this role
35	.. {rbbc_require}
36	HTTP 404 to proceed
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Create
40	{rbtr_director}
41	Service Account*:
42	.. {rbbc_call}
43	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
44	... accountId: `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»`
45	... displayName: `Recipe Bottle Director`
46	... description: `Create/destroy container images for depot ${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`
47	... project:
48	{rbtgi_depot_project}
49	.. {rbbc_require}
50	HTTP 200 response
51	.. {rbbc_store}
52	`«DIRECTOR_UNIQUE_ID»` from response `.uniqueId` field
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Verify Service Account Propagation*:
56	.. {rbbc_call}
57	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
58	.. {rbbc_poll}
59	with backoff until HTTP 200 (newly-created SA may not be immediately resolvable by email)
60	.. NOTE: Poll must use the email address, not the numeric ID. The email-based lookup path has independent propagation from the UID-based path, and subsequent steps (keys.list, keys.create) all use the email address. Polling by UID can succeed while the email path still returns 404.
61	.. {rbbc_require}
62	service account accessible by email
63	
64	//axhos_step
65	. *Preflight: Ensure Pristine Key State*:
66	.. {rbbc_call}
67	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/list[serviceAccounts.keys.list] for `«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
68	.. {rbbc_store}
69	`«EXISTING_KEYS»` from response `.keys[]`
70	.. {rbbc_require}
71	no keys with `keyType: USER_MANAGED` exist
72	.. {rbbc_fatal}
73	if USER_MANAGED keys found: "Manual cleanup required via Console"
74	
75	//axhos_step
76	. *Generate Service Account Key*:
77	.. {rbbc_call}
78	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/create[serviceAccounts.keys.create] for `«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with:
79	... privateKeyType: `TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE`
80	.. Retry up to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX` times on HTTP 404 with `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC` delay between attempts
81	.. NOTE: SA read operations (GET by email, keys.list) may succeed while keys.create POST returns 404 "does not exist" due to write-path propagation delay. Google link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[documents]: "you might need to wait 60 seconds or more before you use the service account." The 404 confirms no key was created, making retry safe. See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
82	.. {rbbc_store}
83	`«DIRECTOR_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` from response `.privateKeyData` field
84	.. {rbtoe_rbra_generate}
85	converts `«DIRECTOR_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` to `«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»`
86	
87	//axhos_step
88	. *Get Depot Project Number*:
89	.. {rbbc_call}
90	link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/get[projects.get] for
91	{rbtgi_depot_project}
92	.. {rbbc_store}
93	`«PROJECT_NUMBER»` from `.projectNumber` field
94	
95	//axhos_step
96	. *Grant Project-Level Permissions*:
97	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} for
98	{rbtgi_depot_project}
99	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with roles:
100	... `roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor` (submit and manage builds)
101	... `roles/viewer` (read project resources)
102	... `roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser` (use private pool for vouch `builds.create`)
103	.. Policy version: 3
104	.. {rbbc_require}
105	IAM policy update successful
106	
107	//axhos_step
108	. *Grant
109	{rbtr_mason}
110	Impersonation*:
111	.. NOTE:
112	{rbtr_director}
113	submits builds that execute as
114	{rbtr_mason}.
115	This requires `serviceAccountUser` permission on
116	{rbtr_mason}.
117	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} on
118	{rbtr_mason}
119	service account
120	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser`
121	.. {rbbc_require}
122	IAM binding successful
123	
124	//axhos_step
125	. *Grant
126	{rbtgi_build_bucket}
127	Access*:
128	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket} on
129	{rbtgi_build_bucket}
130	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with roles:
131	... `roles/storage.objectCreator` (upload sources)
132	... `roles/storage.objectViewer` (verify uploads)
133	.. {rbbc_require}
134	bucket IAM update successful
135	
136	//axhos_step
137	. *Grant
138	{rbtgi_repository}
139	Admin Access*:
140	.. {rbbc_call}
141	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/getIamPolicy[repositories.getIamPolicy] via HTTP GET with `options.requestedPolicyVersion=3` query parameter
142	.. NOTE: {gar_registry} proto specifies GET for `getIamPolicy` with no `additional_bindings` for POST. Using POST fails intermittently with HTTP 404 (transcoding layer rejection, not missing repository). See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
143	.. {rbbc_require}
144	HTTP 200 — 404 means repository does not exist (real error, not empty policy)
145	.. {rbbc_call}
146	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/setIamPolicy[repositories.setIamPolicy] for
147	{rbtgi_repository}
148	.. *Complete policy write*: Add ALL expected bindings to the read policy, regardless of what the read returned:
149	... `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin` — Director manages images
150	... `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.writer` — Mason pushes images at build time
151	.. NOTE: Same complete-policy-write pattern as the secret IAM step. Prevents stale read from overwriting Mason's writer binding set by {rbtgo_depot_levy}.
152	.. {rbbc_require}
153	repository IAM set with all expected bindings
154	
155	//axhoot_typed_output
156	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
157	. *Deliver Credential to Assay Output Slot*:
158	.. {rbbc_store}
159	`«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»` RBRA bytes to the assay output slot via the producer-output discipline (the orphan's `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires)
160	
161	//axhoc_completion
162	Successful execution invests director service account `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»` with build management permissions, generates its credential key, and writes the RBRA file to the assay output slot.
163
