1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_retriever_divest}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation divests a {rbtr_retriever} {giam_service_account} from the {rbtgi_depot_project}.
5	The {rbtr_governor} reads the retriever's installed RBRA, extracts the SA email from the RBRA's `client_email` field, issues a REST DELETE against the IAM API, and removes the production RBRA file from disk to mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse.
6	No arguments are required; the target SA is derived entirely from the kindled regime and the retriever's installed credential file.
7	
8	//axhos_step
9	. *Authenticate as {rbtr_governor}*:
10	.. Execute
11	{rbtoe_governor_authenticate}
12	.. {rbbc_store}
13	`«GOVERNOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
14	
15	//axhos_step
16	. *Read Retriever RBRA and Extract SA Email*:
17	.. {rbbc_require}
18	RBRA file present at
19	{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}
20	.. {rbbc_fatal}
21	if file missing: "Retriever RBRA file not found — retriever may not be invested"
22	.. {rbbc_store}
23	`«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»` from RBRA's `client_email` field
24	.. {rbbc_fatal}
25	if `«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»` is empty or absent: "client_email field missing from retriever RBRA"
26	
27	//axhos_step
28	. *Divest Retriever Service Account*:
29	.. {rbbc_call}
30	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/delete[iam.projects.serviceAccounts.delete] with:
31	... method: `DELETE`
32	... endpoint: `https://iam.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{rbtgi_depot_project}/serviceAccounts/«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»`
33	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «GOVERNOR_TOKEN»`
34	.. {rbbc_require}
35	HTTP 200 response OR HTTP 404 (already divested)
36	.. {rbbc_warn}
37	if HTTP 404: "Retriever service account not found in cloud (already divested)"
38	
39	//axhos_step
40	. *Remove Production RBRA File*:
41	.. Execute
42	{rbtoe_rbra_remove}
43	for the retriever's RBRA file at
44	{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file},
45	mirroring cloud state and preventing stale-credential reuse
46	.. {rbbc_require}
47	file removal successful or file already absent
48	
49	//axhos_step
50	. *Display Result*:
51	.. {rbbc_show}
52	single-line success notice naming `«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»` and confirming divestiture, or a 404-already-gone notice if cloud state was already clean
53	
54	//axhoc_completion
55	Successful execution removes the {rbtr_retriever} {giam_service_account} from the {rbtgi_depot_project} and deletes the production RBRA file, leaving no stale credentials on disk.
56	Proceed to
57	{rbtgo_retriever_invest}
58	to re-issue a fresh retriever SA when needed.
59

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_roster}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation rosters {rbtr_director} {giam_service_account_s} in the {rbtgi_depot_project} via per-SA fact-file emission.
5	Read-only observation: lists all SAs, filters to director-prefixed accountIds, and emits one fact per discovered SA via {buf_write_fact_multi} against the `RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director` extension constant.
6	Fact content is empty (presence-as-fact); stdout assertion of individual identities is retired.
7	
8	//axhos_step
9	. *Authenticate as
10	{rbtr_governor}*:
11	.. Execute
12	{rbtoe_governor_authenticate}
13	.. {rbbc_store}
14	`«GOVERNOR_TOKEN»` from the resulting access token
15	
16	//axhos_step
17	. *List service accounts*:
18	.. {rbbc_call}
19	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/list[iam.projects.serviceAccounts.list] with:
20	... method: `GET`
21	... endpoint: `https://iam.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{rbtgi_depot_project}/serviceAccounts`
22	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «GOVERNOR_TOKEN»`
23	.. {rbbc_require}
24	HTTP 200 response
25	.. {rbbc_store}
26	`«ACCOUNTS»` from response `.accounts[]` array
27	
28	//axhos_step
29	. *Filter to director service accounts*:
30	.. From `«ACCOUNTS»`, retain only entries whose `.accountId` begins with the `${RBCC_role_director}-` prefix
31	.. {rbbc_store}
32	`«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNTS»` — the filtered set; per-role observation per BBAAQ contract
33	
34	//axhos_step
35	. *Emit Fact Per Discovered {rbtr_director}*:
36	.. For each entry in `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNTS»`:
37	... {rbbc_store}
38	`«IDENTITY»` by stripping the `${RBCC_role_director}-` prefix from the entry's `accountId`
39	... {rbbc_call}
40	{buf_write_fact_multi}
41	with three positional arguments:
42	.... `<file_root>`: composed as `<roster_subdir>/«IDENTITY»` (the basename root for the emitted fact file)
43	.... `<ext>`: `RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director` (resolves at source time to `roster-director`)
44	.... `<value>`: `""` (empty content — presence-as-fact convention per RBS0:1081)
45	
46	//axhos_step
47	. *Report count*:
48	.. {rbbc_show}
49	total count of emitted director fact files
50	
51	//axhoc_completion
52	Successful execution rosters {rbtr_director} service accounts via per-SA fact emission — one `roster-director` fact file per discovered identity.
53	The operation is read-only; no service accounts are created or modified.
54

3

30	{rbtr_retriever}
31	Service Accounts*:
32	.. From `«ACCOUNTS»`, select entries whose `accountId` begins with `${RBCC_role_retriever}-`
33	.. {rbbc_store}
34	`«RETRIEVER_ACCOUNTS»` — the filtered set; per-role observation per BBAAQ contract
35	
36	//axhos_step
37	. *Emit Fact Per Discovered
38	{rbtr_retriever}*:
39	.. For each entry in `«RETRIEVER_ACCOUNTS»`:
40	... {rbbc_store}
41	`«IDENTITY»` by stripping the `${RBCC_role_retriever}-` prefix from the entry's `accountId`
42	... {rbbc_call}
43	{buf_write_fact_multi}
44	with three positional arguments:
45	.... `<file_root>`: composed as `<roster_subdir>/«IDENTITY»` (the basename root for the emitted fact file)
46	.... `<ext>`: `RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever` (resolves at source time to `roster-retriever`)
47	.... `<value>`: `""` (empty content — presence-as-fact convention per RBS0:1119)
48	
49	//axhos_step
50	. *Display Summary*:
51	.. {rbbc_show}
52	count of emitted facts; no per-account stdout enumeration
53	
54	//axhoc_completion
55	Successful execution rosters {rbtr_retriever} service accounts via per-SA fact emission — one `roster-retriever` fact file per discovered identity.
56	The operation is read-only; no service accounts are created or modified.

content.text:
## Review of agent work

**All 12 originally-listed repairs landed cleanly.** Verified each file in detail.

### Repair landing confirmation

| File | Lines | Repairs landed |
|------|-------|----------------|
| RBSDK | 162 | NOTE block ✓ · `//axhoot_typed_output` restored ✓ · assay step folded ✓ |
| RBSRK | 106 | NOTE block ✓ · stray `//axhoq_precondition` dropped ✓ · `//axhoot_typed_output` restored ✓ |
| RBSDD | 70 | NOTE block + governor actor ✓ · endpoint inline ✓ · "Divest Director SA" ✓ · redundant require dropped ✓ |
| RBSRD | 59 | "Divest Retriever SA" heading ✓ |
| RBSDR | 54 | NOTE block + `{giam_service_account_s}` ✓ · empty-result branch dropped ✓ · 3-arg `buf_write_fact_multi` ✓ · parallel completion ✓ |
| RBSRL | 57 | `{giam_service_account_s}` ✓ · 3-arg `buf_write_fact_multi` ✓ · parallel completion ✓ |
| RBS0 | (2 sites) | 1086 + 1121 → `{giam_service_account_s}` ✓ |

### New issues surfaced during this review (not in the original 12)

1. **`{rbtoe_rbra_remove}` is undefined.** Appears at RBSDD:44 and RBSRD:42 but has no mapping/anchor in RBS0:155-217. Pre-existing from Stage 2/3 — my repair prompts said "if extant, else inline" but agents kept the broken term. Either define it in RBS0 (with an `[[rbtoe_rbra_remove]]` anchor + definition pointing at the implementation) or replace with inline prose.

2. **Invest step-3 shape drift.** RBSDK step 3 = `"Preflight: Confirm Service Account Does Not Exist"` with `{rbbc_require} HTTP 404 to proceed`; RBSRK step 3 = `"Probe for Pre-existing Service Account"` with bare-prose `"Continue only on HTTP 404"`. Same logic, different headings + different action-verb idiom. Should be parallel.

3. **Divest completion-prose drift.** RBSDD `//axhoc_completion` = 11 lines / 2 paragraphs; RBSRD = 5 lines + a "Proceed to invest" hint that RBSDD lacks. Not parallel. (Pre-existing from Stage 2/3, not on the original list.)

4. **Divest step-4 RBRA-removal phrasing drift.** RBSDD step 4 = "Execute `{rbtoe_rbra_remove}` for the role's RBRA file at `{rbrr_director_rbra_file}`"; RBSRD step 4 adds inline "mirroring cloud state and preventing stale-credential reuse" clause. (RBSDD has equivalent prose in a NOTE block; RBSRD has it inline.) Slight shape drift.

5. **NOTE-block style: inline vs. line-broken.** All 6 repaired files use inline NOTE prose (linked terms interpolated mid-line). Gold-standard RBSDE/RBSDV uses line-broken form (each linked term on its own line). Stylistic — not load-bearing — and the 6 files are internally consistent. AsciiDoc renders both identically.

### State

- **Tree:** 7 files modified, **uncommitted** (per your instruction to agents).
- **All originally-flagged repairs landed.**

How do you want to proceed?
- **A** — Commit the 7 repairs as-is (one commit, scoped to BBAAZ as a follow-up notch); accept items 1-5 as separate work.
- **B** — Dispatch a small follow-on sweep (parallel agents) for items 1-5 before committing. Item 1 is the only load-bearing one (broken linked term).
- **C** — Just fix #1 inline (define `rbtoe_rbra_remove` in RBS0 OR inline the prose), commit the 8 modifications together, defer 2-5 as itch.

Recommendation: **C**. Item 1 is a real defect; items 2-5 are consistency drift that's worth a separate cleanup pace if it bothers you, but doesn't block release.

params.intent: Post-review repair sweep on the cult-verb procedural specs landed earlier this pace. Twelve consistency/correctness fixes across seven files, dispatched via seven parallel sonnet agents (one per file), then a follow-up inline repair on a broken linked term surfaced during review. All repairs are pure .adoc — no code, no test, no behavioral change beyond what already lives in the BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts and BBAAY's depot-identity-collapse vocabulary. Per-file changes: (RBSDK-director_invest) opener converted from prose-style to NOTE: block matching RBSDE/RBSDV gold-standard shape, with {rbtr_governor} as actor and the singleton-per-role + fatal-on-existing semantics re-stated; the bare //axhos_step 'Write Credential to Assay Output Slot' folded into a //axhoot_typed_output {rbtgi_rbra_file} block to restore the typed-output declaration that the orphan had and the Stage-2 rewrite had inadvertently dropped — one //axhoot_typed_output block now exists, anchored on the RBRA file as the operation's typed output. (RBSRK-retriever_invest) same NOTE-block conversion + same //axhoot_typed_output restoration; PLUS dropped a stray //axhoq_precondition block (regime-kindled assertion) that was non-load-bearing and not present in any sibling spec — the regime-kindled assumption is universal for governor-authenticated operations and doesn't earn a callout. (RBSDD-director_divest) opener converted to NOTE: block AND fixed the wrong-actor bug at line 5 — the prose said 'The {rbtr_director} divests the {rbtr_director}...' which was self-divest semantics; corrected to {rbtr_governor} as the actor since divest is governor-authenticated per BBAAQ contract. Replaced «DEPOT_PROJECT_ID» placeholder + 2-line 'derived as RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID from (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER), consistent with {rbtgi_depot_project}' parenthetical with the inline {rbtgi_depot_project} linked term, matching RBSRD's endpoint form. Step 3 heading aligned to cult-verb level: 'Divest Service Account' → 'Divest Director Service Account' (RBSRD parallels with 'Divest Retriever Service Account'). Dropped the redundant post-DELETE {rbbc_require} 'preceding DELETE succeeded (HTTP 200 or 404)' since the prior step's gate already established that contract. (RBSRD-retriever_divest) step 3 heading 'Delete Retriever Service Account' → 'Divest Retriever Service Account' — cult-verb is divest, REST API verb is DELETE; step heading should match the cult-verb level. (RBSDR-director_roster) opener converted to NOTE: block with the defined plural attribute {giam_service_account_s} (was English-suffix plural). Dropped the empty-result early-exit 'No director SAs found / Return success' branch — non-load-bearing scaffolding since the presence-as-fact convention naturally degrades to zero emissions and the final 'Report count' step shows that. The {buf_write_fact_multi} call description rewritten from ambiguous 'with file root, extension constant ..., and basename «IDENTITY»' to explicit three-positional-argument form: <file_root> = <roster_subdir>/«IDENTITY», <ext> = RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director (resolves at source time to roster-director), <value> = '' (empty content per RBS0:1081 presence-as-fact). //axhoc_completion replaced with parallel template matching RBSRL's. (RBSRL-retriever_roster) line 4 plural attribute: {giam_service_account}s → {giam_service_account_s}. The {buf_write_fact_multi} call description had a contract-violating shape — said 'value «IDENTITY»' which would put identity into file content, contradicting RBS0:1119's 'fact content is empty, presence-as-fact'. Rewritten to explicit three-positional-argument form with empty value, mirroring RBSDR. //axhoc_completion replaced with the parallel template ('Successful execution rosters {rbtr_<role>} service accounts via per-SA fact emission — one `<ext-value>` fact file per discovered identity. The operation is read-only; no service accounts are created or modified.'). (RBS0-SpecTop) two sites updated from English-suffix plural to defined plural attribute: line 1086 (rbtgo_director_roster brief) and line 1121 (rbtgo_retriever_roster brief), {giam_service_account}s → {giam_service_account_s} — the mapping at RBS0:200 defines :giam_service_account_s: as the plural form. Inline follow-up after the seven parallel agents returned: surfaced during review that the linked term {rbtoe_rbra_remove} appeared in RBSDD:44 and RBSRD:42 but was not defined in the RBS0 mapping section (rbtoe_* operation entries at RBS0:155-217 do not include rbra_remove). Since the existence of a centralized rbgu_* helper for RBRA file removal is not confirmed in the codebase, replaced both linked-term references with inline prose ('Remove the role's RBRA file at {rbrr_<role>_rbra_file} from disk' / 'to mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse') rather than minting a dangling linked-term that points at no anchor or implementation. The previous {rbbc_fatal} on file-removal failure (RBSDD) and {rbbc_require} success-or-absent (RBSRD) gates remain. Verification: grep -rn 'rbtoe_rbra_remove' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/ returns zero hits; the 12 originally-listed repair items all show as landed (NOTE-block opener consistent across 6 files, //axhoot_typed_output present in both invest specs, plural attribute used everywhere it should be, three-positional-argument form documented for both roster specs, cult-verb step headings aligned, RBSDD wrong-actor fixed, RBSDD endpoint inline, RBSRK stray precondition dropped, redundant requires dropped). Residual drift items judged non-significant for this pace (deferred): invest step-3 shape drift between RBSDK ('Preflight: Confirm SA Does Not Exist' with {rbbc_require}) and RBSRK ('Probe for Pre-existing Service Account' with prose 'Continue only on HTTP 404'); divest //axhoc_completion prose drift between RBSDD (11 lines, 2 paragraphs) and RBSRD (5 lines + 'Proceed to invest' hint); divest step-4 RBRA-removal phrasing drift; NOTE-block style inline-vs-line-broken across the 6 new specs vs. RBSDE/RBSDV (the new specs are internally consistent; AsciiDoc renders both forms identically). These are stylistic / cosmetic drift, not load-bearing or contract-violating; left for a future spec-uniformity sweep when other procedural-spec drift is being addressed.

content.text: 118c2d43333bcba35cb7a6f75cabee41671ad3fb

Repair sweep notched as `118c2d43`. Tree clean.

## Judgment on items 2-5: not significant — no slated pace

Honest assessment of each:

- **#2 Invest step-3 shape drift.** Same logic, different headings + idiom. A reviewer comparing RBSDK and RBSRK side-by-side will read both correctly and won't be misled. Documentation polish.
- **#3 Divest completion-prose drift.** Pre-existing from Stage 2/3, never on the original list. Both completion blocks describe correct behavior; one has a "Proceed to invest" hint the other lacks. Cosmetic.
- **#4 Divest step-4 phrasing drift.** RBSDD has the cloud-state-mirroring rationale in a NOTE block; RBSRD has it inline. Same content, different placement. Cosmetic.
- **#5 NOTE-block style.** Inline vs line-broken. AsciiDoc renders both identically. Internal consistency across the 6 new specs is preserved; the only drift is from RBSDE/RBSDV gold-standard. Purely typographic.

None are load-bearing. None contradict the BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts. None confuse a reader. They don't block release qualification (₣BB heat focus).

**Slating a pace for these would be ceremony.** Would consume officium overhead for ~30 minutes of fix work that has no consumer impact.

Better treatment: leave them as quiet drift. If a future spec-uniformity sweep happens (e.g., during a broader procedural-spec audit), they fold in cheaply. If never, no harm done.

If you disagree, say so and I'll slate; otherwise we're done with the post-review work and ₢BBAAZ is ready to wrap. Want to wrap?

# jjezs_slate spec-cult-verb-procedural-uniformity

## Character

Spec-uniformity sweep across the six cult-verb procedural sub-specs landed in BBAAZ. Pure .adoc — no code, no behavior change. File-disjoint targets → parallel agents. Suitable for landing while long test runs are in flight.

## Drifts (from BBAAZ post-review notch 118c2d43)

1. **Invest step-3 shape drift** — RBSDK and RBSRK use different headings + action-verb idioms for the SA-existence preflight. Pick one shape (RBSDK's `{rbbc_require}` HTTP-404 form is a reasonable target), apply to both.

2. **Divest //axhoc_completion drift** — RBSDD and RBSRD completion blocks are non-parallel (different lengths; RBSRD has a "Proceed to invest" hint that RBSDD lacks). Pick one parallel template, apply to both.

3. **Divest step-4 RBRA-removal phrasing drift** — RBSDD describes the cloud-state-mirroring rationale in a NOTE block; RBSRD has it inline. Align placement.

4. **NOTE-block typographic style** — the six BBAAZ-authored specs use inline NOTE prose with linked terms interpolated mid-line; gold-standard RBSDE/RBSDV use line-broken NOTE prose with linked terms on their own lines. Convert the six new specs to line-broken form for typographic consistency with the rest of the procedural-spec corpus.

## References

- BBAAZ post-review notch 118c2d43 — surfaced these drifts during review of the parallel-agent work.
- RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc, RBSDV-director_vouch.adoc — line-broken NOTE-block reference shape.

## Out of scope

Code, behavioral changes, contract semantics, other procedural specs (RBSDE/RBSDU/RBSGM/RBSDV/RBSDY).
